> MAO ++. That is the enzyme that breaks down serotonin. Can means either high serotonin (aggressive, sometimes short tempered or "prickly") or serotonin swings (moody, with some of those moods aggressive/prickly). Support for this is frequent dosing of very low dose 5htp and/or st johns wort. Some people recommend trying 1/2 cap of a 50mg 5htp capsules 4x a day. I know it sounds counterintuitive to take 5htp for high serotonin (5htp turns into serotonin), but it helps regulate the body's biofeedback (high serotonin turns off serotonin production, which causes swings).

My doctor wants me to take acetly L-Carnitine, and TMG --what do you think of those?

I am glad some are not too severe. I will sprinkle yucca on my protien to take down ammonia.

### [email redacted] — 2012-07-25T04:39:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimizing-methlyation.2658/post-40910>

Supposedly I'm supposed to go easy with the methly donors like TMG because I am COMT+/+. I have not tried the others. What's curious to me is that even though I have CBS, I'm supposed to start NAC and ALA (high in sulfur) to boost my glutathoine levels. We shall see - I have not added those in yet. Next week. I'm debating between yucca and alpha ketoglutarate for the ammonia issue. But will probably hold off few weeks before adding something more.

But we are all different, so listen to your body and see how it responds to the supplements.

This is the vitamin I am using for the MTFR issues: [http://www.douglaslabs.com/pdf/pds/200571.pdf](http://http://www.douglaslabs.com/pdf/pds/200571.pdf) which supposedly has a balance of the Bs and the co-factors needed. In addition, I'm also taking a little folate and a trans-dermal formula with 5-MTHF, mB12, and MSM. I think we are trying to drive all the forms of folate up because I'm just low across the board.

### [email redacted] — 2012-07-25T04:52:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimizing-methlyation.2658/post-40914>

Here is 23andMe / pseudo yasko panel results:

MTHFR A1298C +/-

MTHFR C677T +/-

MTHFR 03 P39P +/-

MTRR R415T +/-

MTRR 11 A664A +/-

ACAT1 02 +/-

VDR BSM/TAQ +/-

CBS A360A +/-

CBS C699T +/-

COMT H62H +/+

COMT V158M +/+

MAO A R297R +/+

Missing from 23andMe are Yasko's rsids for NOS, SUOX, and ACE. And I mis-spoke earlier I do have that pesky MTRR R415T. Darn. Per above - just taking Bs and co-factors to get the methlyation cycle running. Also will be adding NAC and ALA to try and boost the glutathoine.

Before starting the supplements, my methlyation panel was as follows:

**Methylation Panel Results (reference range):**

Glutathione (oxidised) 0.43 umol/L (0.16-0.50)

Glutathoine (reduced) 2.5 umol/L (3.8 - 5.5)

s-Adenosylmethoinine (RBC) 235 umol/L (221-256)

s-AdenosylHomocystine (RBC) 48.8 umol/L (38.0-49.0)

5-CH3-THF 8.8 nmol/L (8.4-72.6)

10-Formyl-THF 3.7 nmol/L (1.5-8.2)

5-Formyl-THF 4.20 nmol/L (1.20-11.70)

THF 0.44 nmol/L (0.60 - 6.80)

Folic Acid 10.2 nmol/L (8.9-24.5)

Folinic Acid (WB) 8.5 nmol/L (9.0 - 35.5)

Folic Acid, active (RBC) 294 nmol/L (400-1500)

Adenosine 17.3 10^3 M (16.8-21.4)

Yikes - LOTS to work on in this space but at least I have some SAMe. I plan to take the supplements for 2 more months before retesting. The question in my mind is should I do any other tests in the interim. I also wish I knew how important the ammonia over-production thing is. I do suffer from a lot of brain fog, but that could be for so many reasons.

### [email redacted] — 2012-07-25T05:08:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimizing-methlyation.2658/post-40923>

Ok - one last note in case it helps anyone out there. When I first started trying to take these Bs my body flipped out. I was dizzy, had a giant headache, felt like I was on the verge of puking all the time. Oh and I got rashes all over my body, followed by strange itchy bumps. They don't call me the rash queen for nothing. So I had to back off and slowly, slowly, slowly build up the amounts. It has been about a month and I'm almost up to a daily dose.

Still get the occasional rash. Still feel a little over stimulated during the day. Feel way too jittery at night which has sleep in the toilet.

When I try to add in the NAC I swell up. Hands, feet and lower legs are puffy. Yuck.

Hoping that this will decrease over the next few weeks. If not I will have to consider if things need tweaking.

### [email redacted] — 2012-07-29T18:38:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimizing-methlyation.2658/post-41804>

On trying to combat the high protein diet with Yucca (per the yasko camp), I did some googling last night. Most of the publications are focused on how this is used for controlling ammonia in large scale farms (pig, chicken) but what I read made me stop and think...

[http://livestocklibrary.com.au/handle/1234/19910](http://http://livestocklibrary.com.au/handle/1234/19910)

It seems that the molecules that do the "ammonia reduction" are the saponins. To which I will have to say no thank you. Saponins are known for causing leaky gut. Cordain has quite a bit of approachable info on this. And it you google saponins and leaky gut you will find a ton of info.

There has to be an another approach out there. Back to the books.

On the AKG this is what I was reading the other day...

[http://www.mothering.com/community/t/1230700/guess-your-genetics-yasko-related/240#post\_15747341](http://http://www.mothering.com/community/t/1230700/guess-your-genetics-yasko-related/240#post_15747341)

from the middle of post #257

_ammonia is high, so is taurine. Meaning, his CBS+ is actually a factor, now that he is digesting protein (Tanya & Shannon, thought this might be relevant for you). He went from red low on ammonia last test to halfway up yellow high this time, that's a huge swing in 4 months. Yasko's solution is a low protein diet. Not going there, so trying some other stuff. I've added alpha ketoglutarate back in - it's great at eating ammonia, although it can produce glutamates in the process. Oddly, for us, at the moment, that would be fine. I'm increasing **curcumin - that's what Yasko uses to push cysteine towards producing glutathione, rather than taurine**.

_

### [email redacted] — 2012-08-09T14:25:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimizing-methlyation.2658/post-43852>

I'm having trouble with the sulfur containing supplements like NAC. I was told that to really understand if I need to restrict protein, I should get the NOS and SUOX genes sequenced.

CBS is the beginning of the transsulfuration pathway and SUOX is the end. If you have an upregulation (CBS) at the beginning and you have a change that causes a slow down at the end (SUOX) the intermediates will collect and your body will be over burdened. The other thing you may be interested in is how well your body can turn excess ammonia into urea. If you have a SNP change in NOS it will impact how well the body detoxifies ammonia to urea.

My MD is not a fan of the sulfur/sulfite or ammonia test strips. He feels that first we need to understand all the key genes and then perhaps look at a Urine amino acid test before making dietary adjustments. Until we know I'm to leave the diet as is and avoid sulfur containing supplements. I don't eat a lot of veggies because my body does not like them.

I'm trying a little experiment where I have protien and veg at breakfast and dinner and then I have something like bulletproof tea or a coconut keto shake as a snack in the early afternoon. I'm thinking about trying a 3:1 ketogenic diet. Right now now I'm probably at 2:1. (grams fat: grams protein + grams carbs).

I found the tekst on high tryptophane foods it was on The heartfixer site.

How best to address the MAO A R297R abnormality is not clear to me.\* As serotonin metabolism is adversely affected, individuals with the R297R defect should avoid large doses of high tryptophan foods (see appendix).\* High doses of St. Johnâ€™s Wort, often taken to address depression, could lead to mood swings as serotonin levels fluctuate.\* Dr. Yasko recommends frequent dosing in small amounts of St. Johnâ€™s Wort, 5HTP (a tryptophan metabolite), and the Mood S RNA formula if serotonin support is needed.\* If serotonin production is impaired on the basis of BH4 deficiency secondary to a Methyl Cycle abnormality, as the abnormality itself is addressed, BH4 levels should stabilize, hopefully normalizing serotonin production.\* \*

Bud I think there is some conflicting information here if you campare it to onther information. Yakso says if you are comt and vdr taq positive like I'm for both +/- it is acting like comt ++ then you should ovoid high thyrocine foods an go for The high trypthophane foods.

What they both don't mention is that when you eat foods with tryptophane The tryptophane stays inactive if you don't combine it with carbohydrates. It are the carbohydrates that are The switch to activafe The tryptophane or the thyrocine. So in my opinion it are not the kind of proteÃ¯ns you eat that make The differance.

It is kind of puzzeling what is best with the CBS mutations. I agree with Ã  lot of what is writhen about it because I can't tollerate NAC msm, Glutathion, glutamine, msg, sulphite, sulphate drugs. At the same time the GAPS diet healed allot of The gut problems And CFS. The leptine reset learned me to eat two or three meals Ã  day. I don't have to carry food the whole day with me, And I'm greatfull for that. But it brought back some CFS things like The tired And wired feeling, The brainfog and some anxiety And more depressed. When I starded The leptine reset some things realy start to get better. My libido came back, I had more enery And was clearer. But after six weeks it slowly wend away. This is like Yakso whrites, you build up sulpher And sulphites slowly in about e few monds.

Counting al The pro's And con's of every diet I found for myself Ã  way to deal with it. I cut out The thiol veggies. I lowered my big as breakfast proteÃ¯ns to one can of sardines or some slices raw salmon; three times Ã  week this is Ã  can of fisch liver. I make Ã  desert of 1 tbs ghee, 1 tbs coconut oil, 1 tsp nut butter Ã  little vanille, lemonskinrasp or orangeoil. 1 tsp raw honey And or stevia. This works for me And I can keep Ã  distanse between meals for five hours or more. If I need something in The afternoon I take only Ã  salad, gapsbouiilon And some coconut oil. In The evening veggies And Ã  little Fisch, organmeat org shells. I notice allready that there is less brainfog end The tired And wired feeling is subsiding slowly.

I call it my gaps/leptin/Yakso diet. I only have SBS -/+ but The mthrf mutations seem to make things worse as my heavy metal toxity. I think for myself when my methylation is working better after proper supplementing, I can upper my proteÃ¯ns again. It is summier now in The Netherlands. In winter I hope I can upper my proteÃ¯ns again.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimizing-methlyation.2658/post-62359>

well I am not feeling so well as my sleep stinks. I can't live off of 3 hours a night. Brain is not working. I have hormones, but clearly don't know if I am taking the right amount as I am on CD 41 and never had that happen before. I am working through a methylation protocol and it is slow going. I have a vitamin a deficiency that I am working on so that my vit. D will work better and also iodine and thyroid. Sometimes I wonder how much weight to put on the methylation vs. brain? What came first? There is not enough information out there on this. I did the BAB last summer for 3 months and then tapered down when I was not getting the results I was hoping for. Started looking at what could be wrong. THen I wen to a hormones specialist which required a flight across the country. That turned out to be a bust after 7 months and a boat load of money. Kept looking and then I took another plane trip across the country and found another doctor who looked at more options and got me on the track of methylation. I am no longer with that doctor, but still on the journey. Trying to piece this together. How can I work with leptin rx and methylation defects. Methylation experts tell me I have to work my CBS gene first and thus lower my protein. I don't like this, but I have been over stimulated to the max and that is what they say too much protein for this gene does. Causes fight or flight mode. I feel sometimes lost in all of this. Contemplating all this while sitting in a tub of ice water after reading BG 16. I am mad and still have a little give a damn left.

### Stavity — 2012-11-19T15:08:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimizing-methlyation.2658/post-65310>

> **freshveggies;67149 wrote:** Methylation or mental illness?
>
>

> [http://metabolichealing.com/michael-s-blog/mental-illness-or-methylation-mutation/](http://http://metabolichealing.com/michael-s-blog/mental-illness-or-methylation-mutation/)

Good article. I just learned something about a close friend of mine who suffers from schizophrenia. She "may" have a copper imbalance. The symptoms fit her to a tee. From this article to another about copper toxicity I learned this: " There exists a high copper personality. Positive traits include a warm, caring, sensitive, emotional nature, often with artistic orientation and a child-like quality. Often high-copper people are young-looking. Many traditionally feminine traits are associated with copper such as softness, gentleness and intuitiveness. This may relate to the qualities of metallic copper, which include softness, malleability and an excellent conductor of electricity.

When the personality is not fully integrated or the copper becomes too high, negative traits show up. These include spaciness, racing thoughts, living in a dream world and naivetÃ©. Other qualities include childishness, excessive emotions, sentimentality, a tendency to depression, fearfulness, hidden anger and resentments, phobias, psychosis and violence. Artists, inventors and other high-copper types often "live on the edge", in part due to their high copper level." The article is here: [http://drlwilson.com/articles/copper\_toxicity\_syndrome.htm](http://http://drlwilson.com/articles/copper_toxicity_syndrome.htm)

This article might also explain why it's harder for some smokers to quit.

When the bible reads, we will never know God from start to finish, it tells me how much we don't know and that we will be learning forever. How complicated "He" made us. I, highly, doubt we are going to be the one's to fix ourselves completely.

### Sujoe123 — 2017-03-31T05:23:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimizing-methlyation.2658/post-211444>

Maybe I am missing something but I am homozygous for CBS 699. I waste B vitamins and my ammonia levels are high. Some doctors believe in limiting B6, but I disagree. I might not know what I'm talking about but there are extremely many other reactions in the body that require B6 and if you've got one reaction using it up then the others are left short-changed. Why not just tweak all the supplements up to as fast as your body needs them. I do the sulfite strips and I'm fine as long as I keep my molybdenum intake up. I use p5p at 50 mg per day in 3 divided doses ( I am homozygous in folate path too...so MF and folinic and M B12) and nibbles of niacin 3 times per day. I supplement with creatine, NAC, glutamine, glycine and P. serine for this pathway....my hair does not fall out by the hundreds but insomnia is horrible due to MAO and CBS snips.

This test will tell you about 35 items and their functional level.
You can repeat it every 6 months (may be 4 months), so withing about a year you should be in control.

Just add Lugols.

I would also suggest getting at LabCorp or Mayo Clinic:

Fatty Acid Profile, Comprehensive (C8-C26), Serum

Creatine Kinase (CK), Total Plus Isoenzymes Test Number: 002154 CPT: 82550 (total); 82552 (isoenzymes)
///

# Thread: Need a place to vent
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-a-place-to-vent.2664/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 20
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Sem — 2012-07-25T08:22:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-a-place-to-vent.2664/post-40951>

So I've been working really hard on my health this year and feel like I'm getting nowhere. I lost about 25 pounds with hcg earlier in the year. Have kept it off. Have cut out sugar, grains, most dairy, doing Leptin Rx. Working in Ct but it is hard!

I was on the Nuvaring and went off of it to try to get a good hormone picture. Got TOM once and haven't since. Doc gave me progesterone to bring it on but so far no luck. Some initial labs showed positive ANA, thyroid in the low end of the range, high cholesterol. Will be going back soon to run more labs but was hoping for period first.

Anyways, I am waking up everyday feeling worse. I have joint pain that been getting worse and am losing hair. Feeling super despondent about t all. I have had really awful luck with doctors in the past and I am so scared that they are going to put me on drugs that will just make me worse. I already got a prescription for statins that I trashed.

I just thought that, after all this work, I would be doing better but I'm not. I am not sure who to turn to or where to go for help. I'm only 42 and I have two little boys. I don't want to wake up everyday exhausted, in pain and sad.

### ealachan — 2012-07-25T12:35:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-a-place-to-vent.2664/post-40954>

I'm sorry you're feeling so rough...\*hugs\*

If your doctor is not treating your pretty obvious thyroid issues, is it maybe time for a new doc? Or are you able to ask for a referral to a specialist, possibly? You shouldn't have to live life feeling this way.

Hi Sem! You are right, it should not be that difficult to find a good doctor but it is. We take care of our families, work and then try to learn so we could improve our health. Don't give up, be a pest if needs to be! I am sure most of the doctors thought of me that I was a crazy lady that was asking for all kinds of labs and wanted copies too!! It took me seeing 3 doctors (some multiple times) to get the labs I needed (a sort of). You need to look for another doctor.

### vkiernan — 2012-07-25T14:09:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-a-place-to-vent.2664/post-40961>

Sem, Sounds like you need more testing and new doc. Good things will happen but you gotta know what to work on or you are flying blind. You may have to pay out of pocket but in the end it will be worth it. You don't have to take the drugs they put you on. It is always in your hand. If you can't find a good doc, keep looking. A lot of us are going on 3 or more in 1 year.

### DarleenMB — 2012-07-25T19:00:57+0300

they can only do that with your permission. Tell them NO synthroid. If you need to go on thyroid meds tell them you will on take the real thing. Remember your doc is not the 'boss' of you. You are PAYING HIM/HER to be your partner in your health care. You have the right to make your voice heard.

Took me a very long time to learn that. And a lot of lost body parts.

I'm so sorry for your pain, it can be so frustrating. I've been fighting Docs for many years now and it is not easy. So many answers here in this forum, very giving people. If you don't have a science or medical background it can be overwhelming at times. I believe one of the secrets of success in life is persistence..just don't give up!!

I wish I had answers for you. One thought I had, if you don't mind posting what area of the country you are in, someone may know good Docs in your area. Hang in there, I second Dr. K....attitude is key.

### colleencoble — 2012-07-25T21:13:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-a-place-to-vent.2664/post-41057>

Darlene is very right. Just go in with a smile and tell the doc firmly that you want Armour, or at the very least T3 added to a dose of synthroid. But you really should get your reverse T3 checked first since you should be on straight T3 if that's out of whack. Your cholesterol will come down, you will feel great. Depression is often caused by low thyroid too. Fixing that fixes a lot of things.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-07-26T00:52:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-a-place-to-vent.2664/post-41103>

Sem, I'm really sorry this is bringing you down. I agree with others that your medical support isn't good and you need good support. This is obvious, but in case you haven't... maybe a consult with Dr K? Since you've got labs, he can be really helpful in pointing you in the right direction, both in terms of what you need from a doc and what you can get doing asap yourself. I totally know the angst of trying to find a doc who gets it (there are a total of 2 functional medicine docs in this country!) but I found the consult so empowering... I am full steam ahead on getting my questions answered and finding someone who can support me in the right way (even if I have to help shape that!). My children are a main reason I choose to fight hard instead of giving up! Hang in there.

Good grief, how awful! I've been vetting docs over the phone... going over some of the questions Dr K outlined in his BHRT webinar and refusing to make an appointment until I hear that the doc is in line with what I'm wanting. (Of course, this has meant finding almost no one who is even in the ballpark, but I agree that we need a good doc and the right Rx!!!) Good luck, Sem. Hang in there.

# Thread: Periclean Athens
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/periclean-athens.2666/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Michael — 2012-07-25T14:39:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/periclean-athens.2666/post-40971>

This a bit off the wall but ...

I was thinking about fish, and I suddenly remembered that fish was _the fashionable food in Athens in the age of Pericles. This was at the time when this tiny city-state went through an intellectual revolution of world-historical importance. Meat seems to have been the staple high-protein food in the Heroic Age, but in classical times it was fish they went for. It had tremendous prestige. See James Davidson's book, Courtesans and Fishcakes_:

[http://www.amazon.com/Courtesans-Fishcakes-Consuming-Passions-Classical/dp/0226137430/](http://http://www.amazon.com/Courtesans-Fishcakes-Consuming-Passions-Classical/dp/0226137430/)

Of course, there would have been all sorts of reasons for the flowering of Athenian society at that time, most of thrm historical and social. But it's kind of interesting that Plato, Xenophon, Thucydides, Aristophanes, and the rest of them would have been eating copious quantities of seafood.

### ealachan — 2012-07-25T15:36:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/periclean-athens.2666/post-40974>

That is VERY interesting.

### nonchalant — 2012-07-25T23:45:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/periclean-athens.2666/post-41092>

I wonder how many times this wheel has been invented.

# Thread: Vacation a la Kruse
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vacation-a-la-kruse.2667/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Dali Dula — 2012-07-25T15:03:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vacation-a-la-kruse.2667/post-40973>

I had a consult with Jack the day before I left for a week vacation on Cape Cod. I had already envisioned a healthy week of eating right , exercising and resting. After my consult i was further inspired. so i went to the sea and ate seafood, all of it , almost every meal. For me that's only two meals a day. Breakfast was cooked at our cottage; eggs , leftover fish from the night before, lacto fermented veg. Dinner was mostly eaten out. Cape Cod is one place where you can go out to dinner and eat not only clean paleo, but epi-paleo. So I enjoyed much fruta de mer. I got lots of sun, used no sunblock, didn't burn. I got dhea cream to bring with me and started using that daily. The ocean temps were in the low sixties which didn't make anyone happy but me. So I had at least 2 CT sessions a day, plus cold shower at night. I biked, kayaked, swam, walked on the beach, rested on the beach. Went to bed shortly after sunset and woke up with the sun.

During a guided Kayak tour we paddled past eroded dunes. In the exposed face of the dune we could see two interesting things.There was a dark horizontal line in the sand that was the original forest floor before the colonists came and cut down all the trees which allowed the blowing sand to bury the forest floor. The other cool thing was an exposed native american shell midden that was many feet thick and 40 -50 ft long. This was where they came to eat shellfish for hundreds if not thousands of years. This was very striking given Jack's recent blogs on human coastal dwelling. Our affinity with the ocean is no coincidence. It is our source.

Well I am done with vacation and I feel really good. I am kind of sad that it seems impossible to live the way we should.

# Thread: How do I change my children's diet?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-i-change-my-childrens-diet.2670/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### itsoversugar — 2012-07-25T16:48:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-i-change-my-childrens-diet.2670/post-40998>

I have 2 sons, ages 7 and 11. We all eat the standard american diet. Ihave type 2 diabetes and for some time now have suspected my oldest son may have it. I took him to the doctor's office with me yesterday to get him checked out. We went over his symptoms (extreme fatigue, increased thirst, dizzy spells), along with some other things he's been experiencing and the dr wrote up an order for bloodwork. I have to take him to the local children's hospital for that.

He is overweight. He weighed in yesterday at 183. I also am overweight, needing to lose 100+ pounds.

I'd like to change our lifestyle as a whole family. Get rid of the processed foods, sugar and junk. My youngest is picky but he does like most veggies. Oldest would exist on all protein meals if I'd let him. How could I do this with kids who are use to eating pure crap all their lives?

Sent from my M886 using Tapatalk

### Shijin13 — 2012-07-25T17:01:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-i-change-my-childrens-diet.2670/post-41000>

> **itsoversugar;41213 wrote:** I have 2 sons, ages 7 and 11. We all eat the standard american diet. Ihave type 2 diabetes and for some time now have suspected my oldest son may have it. I took him to the doctor's office with me yesterday to get him checked out. We went over his symptoms (extreme fatigue, increased thirst, dizzy spells), along with some other things he's been experiencing and the dr wrote up an order for bloodwork. I have to take him to the local children's hospital for that.
>
> He is overweight. He weighed in yesterday at 183. I also am overweight, needing to lose 100+ pounds.
>
>
>
> I'd like to change our lifestyle as a whole family. Get rid of the processed foods, sugar and junk. My youngest is picky but he does like most veggies. Oldest would exist on all protein meals if I'd let him. How could I do this with kids who are use to eating pure crap all their lives?
>
>
>
> Sent from my M886 using Tapatalk

BACON its the gateway meat.

The other thing you can do is talk about why your changing the way your eating - and have a family "spring cleaning" event and throw out all the crap together (Grains, boxed cereal, snacks ect) then go the market together - look at the veggies, fruits, meats and seafood - and talk about why they're good for you and what they can do for you. Have them Identify what looks good to them... on a list. Then go home - don't buy anything at this point.

combine your lists - then look for recipes together on the internet, cookbooks etc and make a meal plan, for Breakfast, Lunch, Dinner

then go buy the foods on that list and when it comes time to make the meal involve them in making it! If all they want to do is eat meat (seafood and grass fed/finished beef and pork) and fat - let them. There are days if my kids don't want veggies they don't have to eat them. Eggs are cheap and easy food. If you can afford pastured get them...

additionally - if they don't eat what's for breakfast, then they have to wait to eat it for lunch, same goes if they won't eat it for lunch it becomes dinner.... eventually if they're hungry enough the'll eat it. Will they bitch, moan, and complain - but you will see a marked improvement in their over all moods and behavior, and eventually they'll be asking you for foods you'd never thought they would...

### KiwiLauren — 2012-07-26T02:07:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-i-change-my-childrens-diet.2670/post-41118>

Your children are about the same age as mine. I think this is a really good age if you didn't start from infancy. What I've found is that they are old enough to understand everything Dr K says, as long as it's put in simplified terms. For example, my kids now laugh and say 'keep your poop where it belongs!' anytime wheat is on offer somewhere. Anytime they felt resistant, I just explained what we were eating/not eating a certain way. Same with using only candles at night or not heating the house. If I couldn't explain the reason, I felt it was my duty to learn and then explain. Dr K's motto is question everything, so their questions were honoured. I work really hard to find foods they like and (though I'm in the minority) don't force them to eat what they detest. But taste buds change and as the months have gone by they now readily eat things they wouldn't eat before (they both now love squid, salmon and soups made with bone broth, for example).

We started by eliminating all legumes, wheat and sugar, except dark chocolate and the occasional treat I make from home with coconut flour and honey. We then eliminated all the other grains. Then almost all dairy except yogurt and the occasional organic cheese. Then we started stepping up the seafood/fish. I also started adding in foods for 'treats' we hadn't had on hand before... toasted pumpkin seeds with sea salt, organic coconut chips (plain, no sugar or oil) so they could have things around that felt like a splurge. We always have olives on hand, too. I have not removed nightshades for them. And they don't eat mackerel head smoothies!

We also aim for a big breakfast, which takes the pressure off the constant hunger for the rest of the day. Both my kids are very active but their lunches are small... we concentrate on breakfast and dinner. Neither liked bacon at first, so we did smoothies (in which I 'hid' raw egg yolks and CO). Now they have a variety of things they like.

The key for us was that BOTH parents were on board AND that all questions were answered. Also, I did not expect them to be happy about the changes. As adults, we struggle with some of the changes we need to make, but it allows for good dialogue.

My kids are now very fluent in terms like melatonin and brain chemistry and understand why we make the choices we do. Their biggest struggle to date is that none of their friends eat this way/live this way nor understand. But as they get more confident (and feel better!) they just smile. As I said elsewhere, my 8 year old has now educated all his friends on why fish head smoothies are super food (even if yucky)!

Good luck.

### Linz — 2012-07-26T11:51:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-i-change-my-childrens-diet.2670/post-41173>

I changed my diet radically seven years ago for health reasons but I kept cooking rice and potatoes for my family and I bought bread for them and didn't say much about what I was eating (I was not sure it would work and my doctor said it would not!). Within months they were all leaving the starches and I noticed sweet snacks in the cupboard were still there and going stale - they still liked them but I think they just didn't feeling hungry enough between meals to go looking for them.

I had always given the children bacon/eggs for breakfast and they did not notice the extra fats (many of the wrong ones at first) in everything else I cooked. The changes in eating behaviour and perception just happened and my daughter was very quick to tell me she had no energy if she ate like her friends when she first left home. I always encouraged them to taste everything without pressure to finish and both ate shellfish happily from a very young age.

I wasn't trying to change anyone but myself and the speed the rest of the family followed me was amazing. If I had been told I 'had to ' change the children's diet first and stop them eating sugar I think I would have failed miserably. There are still some less than healthy snacks here and I never comment if they are eaten but most are not. Seven years ago I wouldn't have been able to resist finishing them all myself - so how could I have kept them from the children.

### Dovid — 2012-08-07T08:38:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-i-change-my-childrens-diet.2670/post-43381>

I have three kids,their diet always chocolate,even like the milk shakes or energy drinks they dislike the bread even not use any vegetable or meat.They are physical so sensitive and soft and hard body wise someone suggest me what should be given them sothat their body change into strong posture.

**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/guernsy-cows-make-a-2-milk.2675/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### chocolate — 2012-07-25T19:27:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/guernsy-cows-make-a-2-milk.2675/post-41035>

[http://www.graceharborfarms.com/Guernseys.html](http://http://www.graceharborfarms.com/Guernseys.html)

### Michael — 2012-07-25T19:41:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/guernsy-cows-make-a-2-milk.2675/post-41041>

That's interesting. I'd heard that most are Guernseys A1 -- although most Jersey are A2.

But at any rate, as long as the farmers has tested the cattle and knows what he's got, I guess which the breed usually is is besdie the point.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-07-25T20:31:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/guernsy-cows-make-a-2-milk.2675/post-41046>

I read somewhere last night that Guernseys had the A2 milk. Agreed on the testing to show which it is would be best!

### Patty Cakes — 2012-07-25T21:06:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/guernsy-cows-make-a-2-milk.2675/post-41056>

"There are three types of casein in cow's milk, called alpha, beta, and kappa. Each type comes in certain variants, depending on the genetics of the cow that produced it. For example, more than 70% of Guernsey cows produce the A2 variety of beta casein in their milk, where 70% of Red Danish Dairy cattle produce the A1 variety of beta casein." The researcher also pointed out that one cow could produce both A1 and A2 because it could receive different genes from the mother and father. Black and white Friesian cows, very common in dairy herds in many countries, have this combination of the two genes.

[www.healthfoodemporium.com/beyond-organic/beta-casein.php](http://http://www.healthfoodemporium.com/beyond-organic/beta-casein.php)

# Thread: You're only as healthy as your cell membrane
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youre-only-as-healthy-as-your-cell-membrane.2676/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Michael — 2012-07-25T19:36:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youre-only-as-healthy-as-your-cell-membrane.2676/post-41040>

Came across this today while listening to an interview of Pam Killeen, who works in addiction therapy.

It struck me as the kind of thing Jack might say.

Here's the interview:

[http://www.blogtalkradio.com/drloradio/2012/06/13/addiction-the-hidden-epidemic-with-pam-killeen](http://http://www.blogtalkradio.com/drloradio/2012/06/13/addiction-the-hidden-epidemic-with-pam-killeen)

It was really quite interesting. Among other things, she seemed to be saying that the damage vegetarianism does starts at the cell membrane.

I was also interested to hear what she said about copper. In her work, Pam tries to balance neurotransmitters, like Julia Ross and others do, but she says she's not found a person witha addiction problems who hasn't also got some degree of copper toxicity.

This is a new one to me, but here's what she says. The level of copper we're exposed to is supra-normal, because we get it through the water supply from copper piping (obviously), from copper sulfate (which is often added as a flocculant by water companies), and, she says -- and this, too, is quite new to me -- because of the fluoridation of water supplies that happens in some jurisdictions, since fluoride ions will grab hold of copper. So what she says is that if levels of zinc are low -- as they are for people on poor diets -- that copper becomes a problem.

It's a fun interview. As well as the what I used for the subject line of this, she had some pretty quotable lines. She said she calls the "four food groups" the "four food lobbies".

And this on talk therapy: "You can't talk someone out of a biochemical imbalance".

### KiwiLauren — 2012-07-26T01:46:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youre-only-as-healthy-as-your-cell-membrane.2676/post-41114>

I listened to that podcast a while back, too. I have also heard Chris Kresser and others talk about copper toxicity and how it can cause serious neurological problems, including psychosis. Another reason to eat epi-paleo!

### Zorica Vuletic — 2012-07-26T02:30:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youre-only-as-healthy-as-your-cell-membrane.2676/post-41121>

I've decided to give up all my supplements in favour of epi-paleo (don't actually KNOW the specifics as I haven't listened to the webinar) however my take on it is much increased seafood with bits of pastured beef and pastured bacon (haha!), fermented veg, fresh veg, lesser amounts of butter and coconut oil (but still use them in cooking) (As he said that too much saturated fat will blunt the assimilation of DHA in the brain...I FEEL WEIRD FOR SAYING THIS B/C IT SOUNDS DANGEROUSLY CLOSE TO CW, HOWEVER I KNOW IT'S NOT THE SAME AND THE EXPLANATION IS NOT CW). Pro-biotic and some inulin from benefibre. This should take care of everything.

### IceBarbie — 2012-07-26T21:03:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/youre-only-as-healthy-as-your-cell-membrane.2676/post-41301>

My former chiropractor who is now practicing Homeopathy, told me how she became copper toxic due to a plumbing issue in her old home. It was awhile back so I'm fuzzy on what happened exactly but it had something to do with something in her well and her copper pipes in combination. So she was taking Zinc to balance the effects of the copper toxicity. I do remember she said it had caused her to gain weight or not be able to lose weight too until she figured it out.

# Thread: blind mice see, kinda like the ion channels and fish
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blind-mice-see-kinda-like-the-ion-channels-and-fish.2681/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### chocolate — 2012-07-25T22:50:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blind-mice-see-kinda-like-the-ion-channels-and-fish.2681/post-41074>

[http://www.psypost.org/2012/07/chemical-makes-blind-mice-see-compound-holds-promise-for-treating-humans-12980](http://http://www.psypost.org/2012/07/chemical-makes-blind-mice-see-compound-holds-promise-for-treating-humans-12980)

> The approach could eventually help those with retinitis pigmentosa, a genetic disease that is the most common inherited form of blindness, as well as age-related macular degeneration, the most common cause of acquired blindness in the developed world. In both diseases, the light sensitive cells in the retina â€” the rods and cones â€” die, leaving the eye without functional photoreceptors.
>
>
>
> The chemical, called AAQ, acts by making the remaining, normally â€œblindâ€ cells in the retina sensitive to light, said lead researcher Richard Kramer, UC Berkeley professor of molecular and cell biology. AAQ is a photoswitch that binds to protein ion channels on the surface of retinal cells. When switched on by light, AAQ alters the flow of ions through the channels and activates these neurons much the way rods and cones are activated by light.
>
>
>
> â€œThis is similar to the way local anesthetics work: they embed themselves in ion channels and stick around for a long time, so that you stay numb for a long time,â€ Kramer said. â€œOur molecule is different in that itâ€™s light sensitive, so you can turn it on and off and turn on or off neural activity.â€


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: What's going on with my thyroid?!?!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whats-going-on-with-my-thyroid.2682/>

**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BJK77 — 2012-07-25T22:52:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whats-going-on-with-my-thyroid.2682/post-41076>

My thyroid is just all over the place. Thankfully my rT3 is staying low, but everything else is totally whacked! The lab couldnâ€™t even test my TSH or T4 this last time due to â€œheterophilic interference.â€ I had to google that to find out that they are some type of antibodies that cause inaccurate readings. I have no idea what to make of that.

The results that they were able to provide are below along with my last labs. The current set was done after starting T3. I had been on Â½ grain in the AM and Â½ grain PM for about 3 weeks prior to this test. Iâ€™m also on Â½ grain Naturethroid AM and PM.

**TSH

7/13: Test not performed due to heterophilic interference

5/15: 4.63 H

2/21: 0.04 L

RANGE: .4 â€“ 4.50 mIU/L

**FT3

7/13: 6.0 H

2/21: 3.0

RANGE: 2.3 â€“ 4.2 pg/mL

**Total T3

7/13: 232 H

5/15: 62 L

RANGE: 76 â€“ 181 ng/dL

**FT4**

7/13: 0.6

2/21: 0.7

RANGE: 0.6 â€“ 1.6 ng/dL

Total T4**

7/13: Test not performed due to heterophilic interference

5/15: 4.2 L

RANGE: 4.5 â€“ 12.0 mcg/dL

rT3**

7/13: 13

2/21: 17

RANGE: 11 â€“ 32 ng/dL

T3 Uptake**

5/15: 36 H

RANGE: 22 â€“ 35%

I thought for sure my T3 was converting to rT3 because after starting Cytomel I was having a lot of difficulty with CT, but apparently thatâ€™s not the case. I am shocked that my T3 is so high. In the past itâ€™s always been extremely low, less than 0.03 even. I donâ€™t have any signs of it being high â€“ no palpitations, anxiety, etc. My sleep has been a bit off lately, but I thought that was more due to hormones. Maybe not though . . .

# Thread: Sugary drinks can change muscles in a month  Read more: http://www.upi.com/Health_New
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sugary-drinks-can-change-muscles-in-a-month-read-more-http-www-upi-com-health_new.2683/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### chocolate — 2012-07-25T23:31:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sugary-drinks-can-change-muscles-in-a-month-read-more-http-www-upi-com-health_new.2683/post-41088>

> BANGOR, England, July 24 (UPI) -- Sugary drinks lead to alterations in muscles similar to those in people with obesity problems and type 2 diabetes, researchers in Britain said.
>
>
>
> Dr. Hans-Peter Kubis of Bangor University in England said the research showed regularly drinking soft drinks changed the way muscles use food as fuel, making them prefer to burn sugars over fats.
>
>
>
> "This study proved our concerns over sugary drinks have been correct. Not only can regular sugar intake acutely change our body metabolism; in fact it seems that our muscles are able to sense the sugars and make our metabolism more inefficient, not only in the present but in the future as well," Kubis said in a statement.
>
>
>
> "This will lead a reduced ability to burn fat and to fat gain. Moreover, it will make it more difficult for our body to cope with rises in blood sugar. **What is clear here is that our body adjusts to regular soft drink consumption and prepares itself for the future diet by changing muscle metabolism via altered gene activity -- encouraging unhealthy adaptations similar to those seen in people with obesity problems and type 2 diabetes."
>
>
>
> The study, published in the European Journal of Nutrition, found the switch to an inefficient metabolism was seen in the participants who were lightly active, lean male and females drinking soft drinks for only _four weeks_.**
>
>
>
> "Together with our findings about how drinking soft drinks dulls the perception of sweetness, our new results give a stark warning against regularly drinking sugar sweetened drinks," Kubis concluded.
>
>
>
> Read more: [http://www.upi.com/Health\_News/2012/07/21/Sugary-drinks-can-change-muscles-in-a-month/UPI-58301342928218/#ixzz21fUhqLRJ](http://http://www.upi.com/Health_News/2012/07/21/Sugary-drinks-can-change-muscles-in-a-month/UPI-58301342928218/#ixzz21fUhqLRJ)
>
>
>
>
>
> Read more: [http://www.upi.com/Health\_News/2012/07/21/Sugary-drinks-can-change-muscles-in-a-month/UPI-58301342928218/#ixzz21fUXOjmC](http://http://www.upi.com/Health_News/2012/07/21/Sugary-drinks-can-change-muscles-in-a-month/UPI-58301342928218/#ixzz21fUXOjmC)

This is pretty amazing.

# Thread: Avocado
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/avocado.2685/>
**Subforum:** Factor X
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### JanSz — 2012-07-26T01:06:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/avocado.2685/post-41106>

Need pointers.

How to buy and store avocado.

How to recognize ripe avocado. Ripen just right, not too much not to little.

Costco sells them, I have mixed luck.

,,

### nonchalant — 2012-07-26T01:13:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/avocado.2685/post-41107>

I get the green ones, and toss them all into the crisper of the fridge. When I want one, I pull it out a day or two ahead of time. Its skin turns dark by that time, and usually it is great inside.

Be sure the ones you buy have a tight skin. If they are loose, the insides will be brown.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-07-26T01:35:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/avocado.2685/post-41111>

once its ripe this is how I eat the fruit.....cut it in half and remove the pit and fill the pit hole with some balsamic vinegar.....and get a spoon and go to town on it. Great meal replacement on the road.

### PrimalPam — 2012-07-26T02:00:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/avocado.2685/post-41115>

One of my favorite dinners is something I call my "Fat Salad". Huge bowl of greens (kale, spinach, etc...after last night's webinar, I'll add sea veggies), 1 whole avocado, crabmeat or shrimp, and drizzled with olive oil or CO and balsamic vinegar. Yum.

### ealachan — 2012-07-26T02:50:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/avocado.2685/post-41123>

> **JanSz;41322 wrote:** Need pointers.
>
>
>
> How to buy and store avocado.
>
> How to recognize ripe avocado. Ripen just right, not too much not to little.
>
>
>
> Costco sells them, I have mixed luck.
>
>
>
> ,,

how to tell they're ripe: go for darker green ones that yield SLIGHTLY to gentle pressure. If they're squishy, they're old and no good. You can also sometimes tell by looking at the narrow end of the fruit - if it has a bit of the stem left, you can try to flick it off with the tip of your thumb. If the stem bit flicks off easily and the spot underneath it is greyish green, it's ripe. If the stem doesn't flick off at all or if the spot is a bright light green beneath the stem, it's likely under-ripe. If it's brown or dark grey under the stem, it's likely old and yucky.

If you buy them under-ripe (ie: hard), you can put them in a paper bag and close it tightly - this will usually ripen them within a day or so. If you cut one open and it's got lots of dark grey streaks, it's not going to be good. Because avocados are primarily fat, they go rancid when they go bad. Yuck!

### BJK77 — 2012-07-26T03:19:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/avocado.2685/post-41135>

> **ealachan;41339 wrote:** You can also sometimes tell by looking at the narrow end of the fruit - if it has a bit of the stem left, you can try to flick it off with the tip of your thumb. If the stem bit flicks off easily and the spot underneath it is greyish green, it's ripe. If the stem doesn't flick off at all or if the spot is a bright light green beneath the stem, it's likely under-ripe. If it's brown or dark grey under the stem, it's likely old and yucky.
>
>
>
> If you buy them under-ripe (ie: hard), you can put them in a paper bag and close it tightly - this will usually ripen them within a day or so.

+1 on all this. I just learned about the tip to flick the end off and check for a good, green color and have been doing this for the last few batches I bought with great success. I also like to get them more under-ripe than over and put them in a brown paper bag. It beats buying rotten, rancid ones.

I had an avocado tonight with dinner. Smashed it up and topped it with sauerkraut with shrimp and mushrooms on the side. Sounds gross, but oh so yummy and totally nutrient dense meal. I could feel my gut healing as I ate it

### ealachan — 2012-07-26T17:04:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/avocado.2685/post-41199>

I just learned about the tip to flick the end off and check for a good, green color and have been doing this for the last few batches I bought with great success. I also like to get them more under-ripe than over and put them in a brown paper bag. It beats buying rotten, rancid ones.
>
>
>
> I had an avocado tonight with dinner. Smashed it up and topped it with sauerkraut with shrimp and mushrooms on the side. Sounds gross, but oh so yummy and totally nutrient dense meal. I could feel my gut healing as I ate it

I like mine very slightly under-ripe, too...and you know, avocado with sauerkraut actually sounds strangely appealing to me at the moment...LOL

### Destiny — 2012-07-27T13:29:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/avocado.2685/post-41483>

> **BJK77;41351 wrote:** +1 on all this. I just learned about the tip to flick the end off and check for a good, green color and have been doing this for the last few batches I bought with great success. ### Sandra — 2014-01-25T07:48:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/avocado.2685/post-106995>

Avocados freeze really well too. Mash them up and freeze them in ice cube trays or just blobs on a plate. Just thaw them out before you use them. Great in smoothies or just blobbed onto salads or as spreads.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-01-25T16:15:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/avocado.2685/post-107017>

# Thread: No self requesting labs available in Australia.

**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jude — 2012-07-26T06:45:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-self-requesting-labs-available-in-australia.2690/post-41162>

Like the title says.....we are just as restricted as the folk in Canada!... .so am looking for other avenues?

Am without a co operative GP... so I'm sunk at the moment (small country town..blah blah). Have app with BHRT Dr in Sydney next month and would like some lab results to take. Some labs in US offer home labs for international clients.

Anyone have any info about how reliable these are? Thanks Jude.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-07-26T07:59:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-self-requesting-labs-available-in-australia.2690/post-41164>

Hi Jude. I don't know anything about requesting overseas labs, but I do know that one doc I found here requests labs out of Australia for functional medicine things! My strategy with my GP (to get some basic labs done) was to say that I'd changed my diet significantly and wanted to make sure I wasn't 'messing anything up' so wondered if he'd order some basic tests... cholesterol panel, B12 and zinc (I was vegetarian making a HUGE switch... even if you weren't previously a vegetarian you could claim you were!), D3 (cause it's winter, ya know), TSH/T3/T4 (cause I'm always cold... these are just the reasons I used with my GP to not trigger any reaction but get the tests ordered), hsCRP, DHEA S. He ordered them all. Then my husband repeated this test with his own GP. He got more resistance, but was able to barter... the GP said he'd order the 'unnecessary' tests if we paid for them.

The only US person I know who I've checked into is Dan Kalish... he tells you what testing he wants you to have and organises it, all by post. Only thing is he is VERY expensive.

### Jude — 2012-07-26T09:05:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-self-requesting-labs-available-in-australia.2690/post-41167>

Just when I thought all these palpitations were reactions to supplements! And, maybe they are?, but she's (GP) totally unhelpful for the optimal journey I'm wanting to take. Have app in Syd next month and just wanted a few test results to take with me. While I'm moaning-fresh fish once a week here-Christchurch looks good!!!

>
> Lauren

### caroline — 2012-07-31T00:31:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-self-requesting-labs-available-in-australia.2690/post-41978>

> **KiwiLauren;41380 wrote:** Hi Jude. I don't know anything about requesting overseas labs, but I do know that one doc I found here requests labs out of Australia for functional medicine things! My strategy with my GP (to get some basic labs done) was to say that I'd changed my diet significantly and wanted to make sure I wasn't 'messing anything up' so wondered if he'd order some basic tests... cholesterol panel, B12 and zinc (I was vegetarian making a HUGE switch... even if you weren't previously a vegetarian you could claim you were!), D3 (cause it's winter, ya know), TSH/T3/T4 (cause I'm always cold... these are just the reasons I used with my GP to not trigger any reaction but get the tests ordered), hsCRP, DHEA S. He ordered them all. Then my husband repeated this test with his own GP. He got more resistance, but was able to barter... the GP said he'd order the 'unnecessary' tests if we paid for them.
>
>
>
> The only US person I know who I've checked into is Dan Kalish... he tells you what testing he wants you to have and organises it, all by post. Only thing is he is VERY expensive.

Hi KiwiLauren. You mentioned you got your Doc to order all those tests - was it covered under medicare? What about HS-CRP and DHEAS ? were they covered as well? When I looked on line - it said that there are only a couple of labs that can do the HS-CRP. Where did you go for the saliva/cortisol panel? which lab in Australia? I am going to my Doc tomorrow. She is pretty agreeable but we will see. I wish I knew more so I could guide her if need be.

Thanks for any info you can give me. Have a great day!

### KiwiLauren — 2012-07-31T00:39:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-self-requesting-labs-available-in-australia.2690/post-41980>

> **caroline;42223 wrote:** Hi KiwiLauren. You mentioned you got your Doc to order all those tests - was it covered under medicare? What about HS-CRP and DHEAS ? were they covered as well? When I looked on line - it said that there are only a couple of labs that can do the HS-CRP. Where did you go for the saliva/cortisol panel? which lab in Australia? I am going to my Doc tomorrow. She is pretty agreeable but we will see. I wish I knew more so I could guide her if need be.
>
> Thanks for any info you can give me. Have a great day!

Hi Caroline. I am in New Zealand, not Australia. I did get hsCRP and DHEA S done and paid for by the national healthcare system. My GP ordered these, with a bunch of other tests (vitamin D, cholesterol panel, electrolytes). I have a history of breast cancer and have never had anyone do any blood tests... so I told my doc "I've totally changed the way I'm eating and living and want comprehensive labs to make sure I'm on the right track." He was ok with that. Though yearly is probably as often as I will be able to get them done because I can't self-order here either. I did not get any cortisol or hormone panels done, but have found a doc who I think will order them, though I will pay. The labs will likely be out of Australia, ironically enough.

### caroline — 2012-07-31T01:18:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-self-requesting-labs-available-in-australia.2690/post-41992>

Thanks Lauren. I think if you check - some naturalpaths will order the cortisol saliva test and a chemist at a compounding pharmacy is looking into it for me. I intended to go back to the naturalpath but they are so expensive and always want to sell you a ton of stuff. She is really into nutrition \[her specialty\] but she is very, very overweight! what gives with that?? I really find I can't bring myself to go back to her. Physician heal thy self and all that.

# Thread: Hack these Labs Please!!!  Very high LDL-P
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-these-labs-please-very-high-ldl-p.2694/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Ray — 2012-07-26T17:56:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-these-labs-please-very-high-ldl-p.2694/post-41204>

My wife just got her first NMR panel done. First the good news:

HDL-C 63 mg/dL (>40)

Tryglycerides 65 mg/dL

Looks good, until you see:

LDL-C 138mg/dL

LDL-P 2311 nmol/L (

### KiwiLauren — 2012-07-27T00:13:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-these-labs-please-very-high-ldl-p.2694/post-41356>

Well, her vitamin D is also super low (should be above 80) and I'm guessing she has inflammation (which an hsCRP would show) and probably thyroid issues, too. She needs more tests to get a complete picture. Has she cut out ALL grains and all sugars? As well as all PUFA sources from her diet? That will create the high LDL. Looks like a classic inflammation-oxidation picture.

### [email redacted] — 2012-07-27T05:04:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-these-labs-please-very-high-ldl-p.2694/post-41448>

Dr Peter attia has an excellent long series on his website about cholesterol, but it is VERY technical, so be sure to use your thinking cap if you go over there to read it. It's at eatingacademy.com.

I've written a few posts on cholesterol, that I hope, or fairly easy for the lay person to understand. I'm not an expert, but I've done a lot of reading and studying. Probably the most applicable post is my most recent one: when good cholesterol does bad things. [http://intoxicatedonlife.com/2012/07/18/when-good-cholesterol-does-bad-things/](http://http://intoxicatedonlife.com/2012/07/18/when-good-cholesterol-does-bad-things/)

I would reiterate what kiwilauren says above. Make sure she eradicates PUFAs, grains, and sugar!

# Thread: Ketosis and seafood
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ketosis-and-seafood.2695/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 20
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Cody — 2012-07-26T18:23:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ketosis-and-seafood.2695/post-41211>

OK, so we know to be in ketosis, it takes a high fat intake. For me, it's upwards of 75%.

So if we are to eat a pound of seafood per day (based on recent blog articles from the doc), and the recommended seafood is mostly protein (and what's up with all the carbs in shellfish???), how the heck am I supposed to do this?

For instance, I buy polar brand canned oysters from Walmart.

1 pound (16 ounces) has the following:

-   cals:560
-   fat:24
    cho:32
    pro:56

To make this work with 75% of my calories from fat, I would need to add 1300 more fat calories. I would need to add 13 TB of butter or 11 TB of coconut oil.

Is this what you all are doing???

Also, any idea how much DHA is in this pound of oysters? Apparently it depends on whether they are atlantic or pacific oysters (with the latter being higher in DHA and EPA than the former).

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-26T18:40:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ketosis-and-seafood.2695/post-41218>

I think your math is wrong. A tablespoon of fat has about 120cals so if you're looking for 1300cals that's more like 10-11T than 21.

I wouldn't worry about how much DHA is in the oysters.... eat your seafood, shoot for the best quality you can, and don't worry about the rest. If you want better DHA absorption have the sat fat away from your seafood.

Oysters are unusually high in carbs and not representative for seafood in general with regards to carbs me thinks.

### quelsen — 2012-07-26T18:44:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ketosis-and-seafood.2695/post-41222>

sounds like oyster stew to me

### Cody — 2012-07-26T18:45:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ketosis-and-seafood.2695/post-41223>

You are correct, a TB of butter is 100 calories, so I would need 13 TB of butter. And a TB of coconut oil is 120 calories, so 11 TB of coconut oil. I'll tweak my original post. Thanks for the correction Souldanzer!

### quelsen — 2012-07-26T18:47:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ketosis-and-seafood.2695/post-41226>

oysters, cream, onion, celery, butter

sounds fine to me

a pinch of nutmeg

### Shijin13 — 2012-07-26T19:02:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ketosis-and-seafood.2695/post-41235>

when I have my seafood (say shrimp) I've been adding extra ghee to it. what ever is left I drink up and lick the bowl

yesterday - we had left over seafood stew - I was still hungry afterwords so I had some liverwurst and then I made Queslen's Keto Shake...nom, nom, nom.

I've been drinking that daily to get me back into ketosis... damn summer light cycles and Carbs (Read fresh berries) threw me from the Ketosis train...

So I'm getting back on... in my 1st consult w/Jack he told me I should be eating btwn 6-8 bricks of Kerry Gold/week! I'm doing my best to achieve that...

### ealachan — 2012-07-26T19:10:06+0300

### kathylu — 2012-07-26T19:32:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ketosis-and-seafood.2695/post-41249>

Well, a lb of seafood per day over 2 or 3 meals is about 5.33 to 8 oz per meal. For me that means I can't eat much in addition. I don't know how you all do it, but if I eat more that about 1800 calories per day, I gain weight, even if it's mostly fat. So for me a ketotic meal meal would be 6 oz fish or shellfish, enough fat to reach 70-75% of total calories and 2 cups of veggies (no more than 10 g carbs per meal). If I do this, I stay in ketosis.

### kathylu — 2012-07-26T19:34:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ketosis-and-seafood.2695/post-41251>

Sorry to mention the dreaded "c" word (calories), but for us LR folks they count big time...

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-26T19:53:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ketosis-and-seafood.2695/post-41260>

> **kathylu;41466 wrote:** Well, a lb of seafood per day over 2 or 3 meals is about 5.33 to 8 oz per meal. For me that means I can't eat much in addition. I don't know how you all do it, but if I eat more that about 1800 calories per day, I gain weight, even if it's mostly fat. So for me a ketotic meal meal would be 6 oz fish or shellfish, enough fat to reach 70-75% of total calories and 2 cups of veggies (no more than 10 g carbs per meal). If I do this, I stay in ketosis.

Leptin rx is about letting go of the calorie counting. Maybe you gain weight so easily b/c you're keeping your body in starvation mode all the time? Maybe your hormones and thyroid aren't up to speed b/c they need more nutrition? Maybe your brain is malnourished?

I guess what I'm saying is that by strictly avoiding the potential weight gain you might not be helping yourself. Calorie restriction is not part of the leptin rx. Eating protein and fat to satiety is.

I'm LR and I would lose weight left and right on your caloric intake.

# Thread: butter/ghee vs coconut oil
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butter-ghee-vs-coconut-oil.2696/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 25
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Souldanzer — 2012-07-26T19:07:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butter-ghee-vs-coconut-oil.2696/post-41238>

What are the advantages of using butter (or ghee for the AI crowd ) over coconut oil?

I've been using CO and coco milk primarily and just introduced ghee. Why am I using ghee now as well? It does take away from my CO consumption. The only thing I know of is butyric acid which is good for the leaky gut. I guess it might have some more vitamins A and K2 and D b/c I use Kerry Gold.

Am I missing something?

Thanks.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-07-26T19:22:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butter-ghee-vs-coconut-oil.2696/post-41244>

I use ghee for the taste. Sometimes I just don't think I can take another bit of CO so I cook with the ghee. I have always said any food is just a conduit to get butter in me. I never even tasted margarine until I was in my 20s. We bought raw milk and churned our own butter from that. I love it! So I cook a lot with the ghee and spoon feed myself the CO to get the goodness of that.

### Lyndra — 2012-07-26T19:35:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butter-ghee-vs-coconut-oil.2696/post-41252>

From what I understand for general use, CO is best used in long light cycles and animals fats (butter/ghee) are appropriate for winter, based on following circadian rhythms. I think Dr. K has suggested CO is good for LR people year-round but don't quote me on that. I, unfortunately, don't seem to tolerate CO, though so many things are going on right now it's hard to say what is triggering migraines.

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-26T20:06:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butter-ghee-vs-coconut-oil.2696/post-41269>

> **Patty Cakes;41461 wrote:** I use ghee for the taste. Sometimes I just don't think I can take another bit of CO so I cook with the ghee. I have always said any food is just a conduit to get butter in me. I never even tasted margarine until I was in my 20s. We bought raw milk and churned our own butter from that. I love it! So I cook a lot with the ghee and spoon feed myself the CO to get the goodness of that.

Tmi alert here..... throughout my AN butter was the thing I craved. The many times I binged there was always ridiculous amounts of butter involved  If I can ever eat butter again in my life, it will be with a spoon.... enjoyed like a piece of very very dark chocolate. Or maybe in combination, how's that for kicking AN in the butt  Holy cow, that sounds good.... dark choc with butter

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-26T20:09:13+0300

So not much purpose for the butter other than taste and a few vitamins? I tend to like tallow for my seasonal animal fats....

I have to do portion size only when I do this

### Michael — 2012-07-26T20:49:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butter-ghee-vs-coconut-oil.2696/post-41291>

> **Souldanzer;41455 wrote:** What are the advantages of using butter (or ghee for the AI crowd ) over coconut oil?
>
>
>
> I've been using CO and coco milk primarily and just introduced ghee. Why am I using ghee now as well? It does take away from my CO consumption. The only thing I know of is butyric acid which is good for the leaky gut. I guess it might have some more vitamins A and K2 and D b/c I use Kerry Gold.
>
>
>
> Am I missing something?
>
>
>
> Thanks.
>
>
> I think all the things you said. Additionally, it's a good source of short-chain fats, which are easy to burn. It's also relatively high in selenium, and has CLA.
>
>
>
> Weston Price used to prescribe cold liver oil with butter oil to many patients, and considered that the two worked together in a synergistic way. It seems to be in particular the MK4 form of K2 in the butter that he was after. In his day it hadn't yet been isolated but he had a sense that "something" was there and named it "Activator X".
>
>
>
> I was interested to see that Ramiel Nagel suggests that skate liver oil might be a good alternative/auxiliary to cod liver oil. He notes that it's a relative of the shark, and says, which impresses me:
>
>
>
> ... in the South Seas, natives would risk their lives to hunt for sharks even though they didn't need to for food. They risked their lives for the oil of the shark's liver. To extract the oil they would store the shark liver in the shark's stomach and let it ferment hanging in the warm air for months.

[http://www.amazon.com/Cure-Tooth-Decay-Cavities-Nutrition/dp/1434810607/](http://http://www.amazon.com/Cure-Tooth-Decay-Cavities-Nutrition/dp/1434810607/)

I guess people don't risk their lives on a whim.

Skate liver oil, he says, as well as being high in A and D has chondroitin, squalene, and alkoxyglycerols.

Green pastures do a fermented skate liver oil. I'ver thought about it, but never tried it.

# Thread: Kidney tumor, recurrent bladder infections
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kidney-tumor-recurrent-bladder-infections.2697/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 47
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### AKMan — 2012-07-26T19:32:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kidney-tumor-recurrent-bladder-infections.2697/post-41247>

Hey guys, just wanted to talk this out with ya'all since you guys are like family...

My wife, 55, 5'2, 120lbs. Skinny her whole life. Lots of medical issues through the years. Interstitial Cystitis, Endometriosis, total hysterectomy 6 years ago due to large teratoma, GERD, IBS, lactose intolerant, stomach ulcers a few years ago.

Around December, she got a UTI and they gave her antibiotics. After 6 months of rounds, she is still getting UTI's and bladder infections. At my urging, she got an MRI a couple weeks ago and they found a 1.7cm tumor on her kidney. She's got a surgery date of 15 Sep to get it removed via DaVinci surgery at the Swedish Medical Center in Seattle.

She's not on board with paleo or CT, but since I'm the cook, I make sure she eats pretty good. She has always had a sweet tooth and gets lots of calories from sugar. I can't control everything she eats, but I do dog her a bit hoping she will see the folly of her ways.

I was reading about Apple Cider Vinegar for bladder infections and bought her a bottle yesterday. I think she needs to get over this infection problem before her surgery, but I really don't see how she can. We have bone broth a couple times a week, but she is such a picky eater with her Lactose Intolerance, GERD and IBS that anything out of her comfort zone is hard for her to eat. Most green veggies are out, she hates seafood, she eats lots of chicken, rice, and venison.

It's hard for me to watch this all unfold, because I can't see it ending well. Any thoughts?

### Shijin13 — 2012-07-26T19:54:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kidney-tumor-recurrent-bladder-infections.2697/post-41261>

Sorry you and your wife are going through this. DH had a very similar perspective on paleo/CT etc, until I sent him to Jack for a 2nd opinion on his back. Lets just say - spending the 30-45min DH spent w/Jack was eye opening for him. he came back insisted we throw away all grain products, and he's on 100% on board w/Epi-Paleo WOL.

If you can swing it - I would recommend you have your wife do an educational consult w/Jack BEFORE the surgery, then do a follow up w/him once you know the status of the tumor.

While its hard to watch your loved one's make decisions that hurt them, they've got to want to make the change. And lets be honest - no spouse is ever good at taking direction on changing their ways from their other half. DH wasn't going to listen to me, wouldn't listen to our Chiropractor, but he did listen to Jack. He thin slices the info - so you understand it, and you can determine what you're going to do to fix it. Understanding what's really at stake, can make you change quite quickly....

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-26T20:30:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kidney-tumor-recurrent-bladder-infections.2697/post-41280>

I'd say the extended use of abx killed the good flora and now she's vulnerable for recurrent infections.

Probiotics would be my point of attack, obviously, that won't help much if she also eats tons of sugar, now has an even worse leaky gut from the abx.... etc. I think something like this can be the breaking point for some of us. We're "fine" until we do something that entirely throws us off and then our underlying issues start staring us into the face until we can't ignore them any longer.

Cranberry helps with UTI's, too.

### indigogirl — 2012-07-26T21:23:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kidney-tumor-recurrent-bladder-infections.2697/post-41307>

Hi Akman,

Sorry your wife and you are going through this.

I had frequent UTI's for years. TMI, but apparently the diaphragm I was using for birth control was not helping. I took one antibiotic (first macrodantin, then switched to Trimethoprim) post coitally for years to prevent infections.

Cranberry juice and D- Mannose did not help me.

Other than being 80% Paleo ( I still have a sugar addiction) and religiously taking probiotics I stopped taking the antibiotics about a month ago and I am not getting UTI's. EDIT: Souldanzer reminded me that in his recent Webinar Jack said fermented veggies (kimchee, sauerkraut, and pickles) are a better way to get probiotics than from a supplement.

Don't know if any of the above will be useful to your wife...

I think Shijin's advice is good, and that a consult with Jack will be helpful.

Best of luck with all this.

Edit...D-Mannose didn't help me, but maybe it will help your wife:

[http://www.d-mannoseworks.com/](http://http://www.d-mannoseworks.com/)

[http://www.squidoo.com/d-mannose](http://http://www.squidoo.com/d-mannose)

Can't find a link...Whole Foods had a D- Mannose powder supplement to take during a UTI...it looked good.

### nonchalant — 2012-07-26T22:13:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kidney-tumor-recurrent-bladder-infections.2697/post-41317>

AKMan, sorry to hear of her troubles. For the UTI, and the tumor as well, perhaps she can agree to avoid fructose? DH has switched to sodas without HFCS successfully, and he's making other progress elsewhere.

I agree about the consult with Jack. He seems to really be able to convince people of the error of their ways. I want him to consult with some of my family for that reason. Hopefully they won't be in a food-opiate haze or a sugar high when they do talk to him.

### kathylu — 2012-07-26T22:45:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kidney-tumor-recurrent-bladder-infections.2697/post-41328>

I can't say much about the UTI except that cranberry extract helped me the one time I had one.

Hopefully your wife's tumor will be benign.

But....please go get a copy of Suzanne Somers 2008 book "Knockout". You read it immediately, then give it to your wife if needed. She interviews M.D.'s who are using alternative means to cure cancers of all types. **It's a real eye-opener.** Even for people who would never consider alternative treatment there are chapters on how to make conventional treatment more effective and easier to tolerate. There is also great info on prevention.

Actually, I take back the "if needed" above. Everyone needs this book.

### AKMan — 2012-07-26T23:14:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kidney-tumor-recurrent-bladder-infections.2697/post-41338>

Thanks, guys. I will order "Knockout" today. Hopefully the kidney tumor isn't cancer. 5 years ago it was 1.3mm, now 1.7cm so they want to take it out no matter what.

I was doing really good working on healing her gut with foods she could eat, bone broth, no artificial sweeteners, and weaning her off sugar...then the uti's hit hard. I think her gut is so leaky from years of poor nutrition and antibiotics that it will take a while, but she goes back to comfort foods when she's not feeling well (cookies, red licorice, cereal, hard candies). I tried sneaking kale in a few weeks ago and she was on toilet all night--she's really sensitive to cruciferous and green leafy veg in general.

She's had several rounds of skin cancer in past 10 years, her father died of lung cancer (smoker) last year, mother is 75 and very poor health (lupus, bronchitis, also smoker). Wife has never smoked, luckily.

Have any of you ever heard of Apple Cider Vinegar for UTI's? Lots of anecdotal evidence on web that it works by changing ph of urine.

### nonchalant — 2012-07-26T23:41:54+0300

### KiwiLauren — 2012-07-26T23:56:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kidney-tumor-recurrent-bladder-infections.2697/post-41354>

> **AKMan;41556 wrote:** Thanks, guys. I will order "Knockout" today. Hopefully the kidney tumor isn't cancer. 5 years ago it was 1.3mm, now 1.7cm so they want to take it out no matter what.

I think her gut is so leaky from years of poor nutrition and antibiotics that it will take a while, but she goes back to comfort foods when she's not feeling well (cookies, red licorice, cereal, hard candies). I tried sneaking kale in a few weeks ago and she was on toilet all night--she's really sensitive to cruciferous and green leafy veg in general.
>

>
