The tail end of this article sounds just like your problem to me. I might not understand it correctly.

[http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Na%2B/K%2B-ATPase](http://http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Na%2B/K%2B-ATPase)

> Within the last decade, many independent labs have demonstrated that, in addition to the classical ion transporting, this membrane protein can also relay extracellular ouabain-binding signalling into the cell through regulation of protein tyrosine phosphorylation. The downstream signals through ouabain-triggered protein phosphorylation events include to activate the mitogen-activated protein kinase (MAPK) signal cascades, mitochondrial reactive oxygen species (ROS) production, as well as activation of phospholipase C (PLC) and inositol triphosphate (IP3) receptor (IP3R) in different intracellular compartments.\[4\]

# Thread: more on sweating and Leptin reset
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/more-on-sweating-and-leptin-reset.2880/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 17
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### somewhatchubby — 2012-08-06T17:17:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/more-on-sweating-and-leptin-reset.2880/post-43212>

hello, I have just been reading about changes in sweating that are epxerienced with the Leptin Rx . From what I read most people seem to be profuse sweaters and hope to become less profuse after the reset. What does it mean if you dont sweat much at all ? is it a sign of being Leptin sensitive even if you are overweight ? Or does the pattern of sweating itself change that is non sweaters start to sweat and profuse sweaters sweat less.

Why I ask this is that my husband who is nearly 64 and almost 20 stones (280lbs )really dosnt sweat and never has.

He has no major health issues apart from intermitant discomfort with an benign enlarged prostate,he has a good history of BP and is not diabetic . (yet )He has had low pressure Glaucoma for a number of years .

He has a basic good diet and always has, meat, butter,home grown veggies but not much fruit but eats a lot of potatoes because he grows his own and likes them, quite a bit of bread ,3 teaspoons sugar in a mug of tea ( 5 x daily) and eats cakes and biscuits and pastry and rice and ice cream. I do not buy these things he buys his own ! despite gently suggesting that it might be agood idea to cut down on these things he has accused me of nagging !

Last winter he developed mucus on his chest and was coughing first thing in the morning, he went to the docs who did some tests and said he wasnt concerned but as he hasnt smoked for over 35 years I felt this wasnt right.

Just recently, I think he has done some soul searching, , he has become ungainly. his joints and muscles ache and he is very lethargic. He has alwasy been an active and hard working man.

So, I have got him to give up his sugar, milk ,cakes ,etc basically very low carb except for a half pint of beer with his evening meal. ( Yes, I know, liquid bread !) If I push him too far he might well not stay the course.

Will what he has agreed to give up be of some benefit to him and is it posible that he may become LS on his current regime.

thanks for any input.

### AKMan — 2012-08-06T23:12:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/more-on-sweating-and-leptin-reset.2880/post-43278>

I'm going through the same kind of thing with my wife. Trying to break someone of a lifetime of bad eating habits is hard if they don't want to do it. People refuse to connect the dots between their food choices and their health.

As to the sweating, I wouldn't worry too much about it. What has been said is that most people see a change in their sweating patterns when becoming leptin sensitive. I used to sweat profusely when overweight, went to being a complete non-sweater after doing the leptin reset and getting my weight under control, to now, over a year later, sweating like a 'normal' person, ie. when exerting myself or when hot--but not profusely.

### somewhatchubby — 2012-08-06T23:38:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/more-on-sweating-and-leptin-reset.2880/post-43286>

Hi AKman , how right you are. its easy to make your own choices but when someone close to you is obviously going the wrong way and knowing that you are not in control but relying on their good sense to kick in.

I wish you luck with persuading your wife . I hope that when my husband starts to feel better agin he will be spurred to continue.

### AKMan — 2012-08-07T00:24:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/more-on-sweating-and-leptin-reset.2880/post-43298>

> **somewhatchubby;43573 wrote:** ...relying on their good sense to kick in.

Just spent the entire weekend with my mother-in-law. 75, long-time smoker, eats candy all day and drinks Ensure (a 'healthy' alternative to eating). She has lung disease (COPD) and is in the hospital because she couldn't catch her breath. While there, they thought she had a heart-attack due to abnormal EKG and they did an angiogram to look for heart artery blockages, she had none, but does have a weakened heart muscle and leaking mitrial valve. She is pretty much bed-ridden, house-bound for sure. All of her problems stem from choices she made over the last 10 years--smoking and eating.

Her husband, 75, works every day, eats fairly well but still SAD. Non-smoker, in pretty good shape, but lots of age-related illnesses like osteoporosis, arthritis, etc...

I keep telling my wife she doesn't have to end up like that, at least she doesn't smoke!

### Inger — 2012-08-07T09:02:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/more-on-sweating-and-leptin-reset.2880/post-43386>

I know how you feel. My skitzofrenic brother that lives in this house too.. I helped him so much last months, I really pulled him up and he ate the epi-paleo way, did CT and such.. got better pretty fast.. but then all of a sudden he got influenced by some friends or in the internet and now for a few days already, he is a vegetarian, eats bread.. believes it is totally wrong to eat animals.. I can tell it hurted me to see that. He listened to some old friends convincing him I am quite sure (because he has this old paramour she is a religious vegetarian) or then he read something on the internet. I could just not control what he feeds his brain with (thoughts and reading, friends). That would have gone too far. So I needed to give him this freedom and yes, that is what happenend. Now he is so much worse off, just in a few days. It hurts for sure. But then I am thinking, in the end I cannot care for anybody else that myself, truly. If someone really wants, that is a different case. That is the only way it can work, you have to really want to get well. I mean really...

I learned something though, through all this.. even if it is kind of bittersweet to have wasted all this time and energy.. I learned to quit try helping. I truly do not care what others do with their life. Some just want to suffer, and that is their desicion. I do not feel sorry for them. Maybe this is not a good thing but that is the only way I manage to do it right now, or I will loose all my power that I need for my own healing. Worryimg for someone who is in pain, but who do not want to change - is wasted energy.

LOL...I just today had an email exchange with a friend who said the old "But cavemen didn't live as long as today's man; must be that today's man eats a better diet (graiins, etc).

I replied: "Go to youtube and watch Terry Wahls M.D.'s video. Sure, she could live a lot longer, but in a tilt-reclined wheelchair or horseback riding? You decide".

I went on to say that today's man doesn't have to become the hunted by its prey; our prey are neatly packaged at the grocery store. So animal mauling deaths are way down. Also, all those drugs they use to keep you alive (like AKMan's MIL)? I asked my friend to spend a day at a nursing home and decide if that's how you want to live your life.

Gotta just change yourself and if people are truly interested, then the teacher in us will appear.

Hi, I am 59 ,nearly 60 female, history of losing the same 14 lbs of weight year in year out. Have tried Atkins but it did nothing for me. In January this year I tried to get rid of the same old stone and after much research ended up here via MDA.

Despite doing low carb to the letter I have gained weight slightly and when i read a comment on LR on MDA it resonated within me. It said if you are lacking in energy and spent the day laying around or words like that. I thought ,yes, this is me and proceeded to read as much as I can about Leptin and its affect on us.

Since starting I have lost that 'wading through glue' feeling , foggy thinking, lethargy and tired muscles despit not having done much to cause it.

Anyway, I am in my first week and my hubby just two days in. I have about 1 stone to lose but its been a struggle so far and I hope that once my system is optimised I might be able to do that easier.

OK< off to read the many posts now.

# Thread: Drinking too much.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/drinking-too-much.2884/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Raiken3712 — 2012-08-06T19:08:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/drinking-too-much.2884/post-43235>

I just can't figure out this water issue..I've heard clear urine = too much...and also that its fine. I drink quite a bit...I'm not good at telling when I'm actually thirsty..which Mercola says is when you should drink.

Any tips on good drinking amounts? I had a liter between 9-10 in the morning and am almost done with another by 11 ...prob a bit much. Hard to tell what I need.

### Michael — 2012-08-07T08:35:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/drinking-too-much.2884/post-43380>

AFAIK, the figure of two liters comes from a rough estimate of what one's likely to get in all drinks plus all foods throughout a day.

But some people seem to have battened on to it and recommended it as the amount of water one needs to drink a day.

As a prescription rather than an estimate of an average that seems doubtful ... since, anyway, the amount of fluids you might want or need is going to depend on your size, what you're doing, and how hot it is. So how could one put an exact figure on it that should be appropriate for all people at all times in all places?

Depending on all the above, a couple of liters, in addition to what's in your food and drink, might be excessive or might not be enough. There was a TV series of Bear Grylls training with the French Foreign legion in North Africa. IIRC, doing punishing training out under the sun, the soldiers are made to drink 10 liters of water a day. And it's a punishable offence if you don't (since you could make yourself non-effective, and since it could be done as self-inflicited damage to dodge the work).

[http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=pQhFFhF6eUA\[/video\]\]\[video=youtube\_share;pQhFFhF6eUA\]http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=pQhFFhF6eUA\[/video\]](http://[video=youtube_share;pQhFFhF6eUA)

So you say you've drunk a couple of liters by 11 o'clock? Yes, that does sound quite a lot, if you're indoors and not exercising -- especially if you weren't thirsty and didn't want it. I would think that if your urine is clear that indicates that you haven't got any excess electrolytes to get rid of (or anything else that would colour it) and could possibly now not have enough electrolytes.

The other thing to consider would be frequency of urination. If you need to keep running to the bathroom to get rid of it, then perhaps you're drinking too much.

There's an old Sean Croxton Underground Wellness podcast on water-overconsumption. Now the guy that Sean's interviewing is a bit eccentric and somewhat notorious in the Paleo Community as a high-carb proponent -- and here he recommends drinking fruit juice diluted only 1 to 1 (!) -- however, I tend to think he probably does broadly speaking make his case here:

[http://www.blogtalkradio.com/undergroundwellness/2012/02/22/the-dangers-of-our-water-obsession-with-guest-matt-stone](http://http://www.blogtalkradio.com/undergroundwellness/2012/02/22/the-dangers-of-our-water-obsession-with-guest-matt-stone)

_Caveat_: I'm not medically qualified, nor have I tried to get hold of and read any of the literature that discusses any relation between particular symptoms and excessive water consumption.

I know you're living in a hot area of the country and I don't want to encourage you not to drink enough. But if you have to urinate frequently and the urine isn't coloured, then it sounds like you're taking in more than you need.

### Raiken3712 — 2012-08-07T18:16:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/drinking-too-much.2884/post-43438>

Yeah I think I am...more typical is 1 liter from 3 when I get to work till 6-6:30 for lunch...I call it lunch ...its dinner..its weird but I always call meal at work lunch. Than I drink a liter from 6-6:30 till 10:30 when I get off.

I do tend to go to the bathroom quite a bit...hard to figure out how much is good. Guess I'll just have to experiment a bit. I think I also need to make sure to add some Real Salt to my water at home/work to keep my electrolytes up. Not a ton just a little bit...to make sure I get plenty of sodium. I recently got the book The Art and Science of Low Carbohydrate Living and it brought up the fact that carbohydrate restricted diets require more sodium as the kidneys dump sodium.

I forgot why they are said to do this but I've read it before. I feel tired at times...and that may be the cause as ease of fatigue is one of the symptoms of low sodium...or at least one of the causes. Other possible issues...there are tons.

Urinating frequently is also tied to diabetes....I was testing in the 130s range...and my doctor from the paleo physicians network who follows Paleo labeled this diabetic. I'm currently in the fasting range of the 80s...but perhaps am still somewhat insulin resistant ...not sure what causes extra urination in diabetes...maybe some of it is related to that. Not sure if its all due to drinking too much or maybe that too...not sure.

### ealachan — 2012-08-07T19:44:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/drinking-too-much.2884/post-43459>

I have a hard time knowing how much to drink too, as I often have a dry mouth / throat, even when I'm not thirsty per se. I usually drink 2ish liters during an 8-hour work day, then a glass or two more at night, plus my coffee in the morning and the water I take my supps with...so I'm probably close to 3 liters a day, total.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: CT milestone
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-milestone.2885/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### msclman — 2012-08-06T19:30:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-milestone.2885/post-43238>

Had a new record. 44 degree's 55 minutes. first time i just couldnt take anymore. i put on swim goggles and for the last 10 minutes do breath holding submerging my head. have to say, i dont know if its the hot weather or not...but i crave the cold water now and get to 30 minutes b4 shivering

# Thread: What does Jack mean in Ask Jack answer?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-does-jack-mean-in-ask-jack-answer.2895/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### [email redacted] — 2012-08-07T00:54:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-does-jack-mean-in-ask-jack-answer.2895/post-43311>

Hi All,

I thought I would try you in case I'm being dense. I'm not sure what he's saying as a response to my ask jack question! I don't want to ask him again if someone else understands the answer?

[http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?3039-If-you-are-utlizing-the-food-where-is-it-going](http://http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?3039-If-you-are-utlizing-the-food-where-is-it-going)

### chocolate — 2012-08-07T01:15:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-does-jack-mean-in-ask-jack-answer.2895/post-43314>

I think he is saying particular flora are particular to the gut geography. I kind of learned that from sibo. Then each surgery is different and different parts get messed with. The anti depressants the gut makes tends to be very disturbed. The iron absorbing section does also. There are articles posted. I'm personally still stuck on the Peyers Patches a few Gut/Brains back because I think my personal situation is dominated by arachadonic acid and we've had circadian wasting, like HIV patients.

### [email redacted] — 2012-08-07T17:27:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-does-jack-mean-in-ask-jack-answer.2895/post-43429>

Chocolate - bless you, but I just don't understand you either! Now, I freely admit it is probably me that is failing on the "receiving" communication end.

So, my question (for those of you that want to take a stab!) was:

When someone is not "absorbing" all the food due to bypass - what is happening with it? It seems like it doesn't just get pooped out, so is it stored as fat, and one just gets more fat without all the nutrients being absorbed?

### Melinda — 2012-08-07T19:29:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-does-jack-mean-in-ask-jack-answer.2895/post-43452>

I'll take a stab...the bacteria in the small and large intestines eat it. They can turn it into fats that you might absorb. But mostly it just turns into more of "them" that ultimately get pooped out.

### [email redacted] — 2012-08-07T20:01:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-does-jack-mean-in-ask-jack-answer.2895/post-43462>

Jack answered this in comment #50 IN BG7. The adiposity index of certain gut flora can cause weight gain because they extract more calories from the food.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-08-07T20:10:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-does-jack-mean-in-ask-jack-answer.2895/post-43468>

If you gut has been altered it means by definition so has your gut microbiota.......and once that happens there is no way to know what the hell your gut bacteria is doing now with an altered gut because it is no longer following normal human endobiome patterns......it has been permanently altered by a surgeon. This means that it will be hard to figure out.......but guess what the brain does in this case? It rewires within a yr......and figures it out for you. this is why most weight loss ceases after a year in a by pass.

### caroline — 2012-08-09T06:37:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-does-jack-mean-in-ask-jack-answer.2895/post-43824>

> **Cavemam;43720 wrote:** Chocolate - bless you, but I just don't understand you either! Now, I freely admit it is probably me that is failing on the "receiving" communication end.
>
>
>
> So, my question (for those of you that want to take a stab!) was:
>
>
>
> When someone is not "absorbing" all the food due to bypass - what is happening with it? It seems like it doesn't just get pooped out, so is it stored as fat, and one just gets more fat without all the nutrients being absorbed?

I was so happy to read that - I didn't understand either answer as well! But Dr. K. has now made it very clear. Thanks

# Thread: Inger will you donate your poo?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/inger-will-you-donate-your-poo.2896/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 18
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### [email redacted] — 2012-08-07T01:28:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/inger-will-you-donate-your-poo.2896/post-43315>

I really want to ID an optimal FT donor (poo swap if you will).

I bet getting it done soon might not be out of the questions - at least before the FDA catches on and starts regulating it.

Inger?

I wonder if you could transplant some obese people with anorexic bacteria and they would start to be anorexic.

[http://fecalmicrobiotatransplantation.com/FMT/home.html](http://http://fecalmicrobiotatransplantation.com/FMT/home.html)

### [email redacted] — 2012-08-07T01:44:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/inger-will-you-donate-your-poo.2896/post-43316>

I am being serious about who would make an ideal donor - I suppose it would be different for each person - since this is the beginning of this therapy, it's all shooting in the dark.

Naturopaths can do a enema version, but I bet something more sophisticated that gets bacteria all up in there is right around the corner.

Anybody know who's on the cutting edge of this? The Portland clinic linked below seems to get the most press.

### nonchalant — 2012-08-07T02:16:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/inger-will-you-donate-your-poo.2896/post-43319>

This would be a great way to pay for regular labs, once we are healthy.

### Destiny — 2012-08-07T02:26:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/inger-will-you-donate-your-poo.2896/post-43322>

LOL ....Or you could sell it on E-Bay!

### Patty Cakes — 2012-08-07T02:51:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/inger-will-you-donate-your-poo.2896/post-43326>

OMG, I have to follow this thread because it makes me laugh! But also I want to know for my own personal situation. Maybe we could get a donor and get a group discount for insertion...at least those of us who have massively screwed up floras.

### Souldanzer — 2012-08-07T02:54:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/inger-will-you-donate-your-poo.2896/post-43329>

And I wonder if you would really gain any benefit from that

I will never get why so many people think anorexics somehow have it easier than obese people, which appears to be the common consent.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-08-07T02:59:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/inger-will-you-donate-your-poo.2896/post-43330>

my GI doc and I have actually thought about offering this service. He is a bit gun shy as yet......

### [email redacted] — 2012-08-07T03:48:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/inger-will-you-donate-your-poo.2896/post-43343>

> **Souldanzer;43616 wrote:** And I wonder if you would really gain any benefit from that
>
>
>
> I will never get why so many people think anorexics somehow have it easier than obese people, which appears to be the common consent.

Also, It's been said by many sciency types "it's a damn good thing you were never in a position to experiment on people". . .

### [email redacted] — 2012-08-07T03:50:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/inger-will-you-donate-your-poo.2896/post-43344>

Doc, bring him a group of patients/volunteers for initial experiments! Also, I know Inger will read later - but some kind of protocol/standard of testing and identifying ideal donors.

If Inger were suitable donor, perhaps we could all chip in to bring her over for a little American Adventure - powered by poo!

### [email redacted] — 2012-08-07T03:51:40+0300

### Souldanzer — 2012-08-07T03:56:02+0300

problem with that is that AN doesn't exist not in the extreme. It's a disease of extreme. Anyways. I get what you mean but it's a stab anyways every time someone says something like this.

I think I'm going to choose to fix my own poo w/ regards to the topic of the thread.

### freesia — 2012-08-07T04:12:00+0300

### kathylu — 2012-08-07T07:43:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/inger-will-you-donate-your-poo.2896/post-43376>

Here's a DIY (do-it-yourself) protocol for fecal transplant. I'm talking to my husband about trying it (him the donor, me the recipient). He is massively unconvinced at this point, but says he certainly has poo to spare!

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/20117243](http://http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/20117243)

### Inger — 2012-08-07T08:11:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/inger-will-you-donate-your-poo.2896/post-43378>

I almost got a shock coming here for reading today... I thought, WHAT....?!

But then I realized this is for real and I do knew about the poo-transplants already. I guess they do them in Europe because I have learned about it reading stuff about bowel-treatments and such. They do them in hospitals when people have a really bad gutflora and it is very effective I think. Sure it sound a little gross..

I need to quit eating anisakiasis-infected mackerels then, cause I guess you do not want my little worms do you Cavemam?  (I do guess they have all died in my stomach because I have seen none in the toilet.. and I have no digestion-troubles eather)

But honestly I have no problem doing it, just send a little poo in a package.. humm. Sound strange though.

I need to get more optimal! I am feeling great but I want to give you only the best poo, you know! Oh my.. this sounds so surreal.. Give me a few weeks and I will optimize my gutflora with all I ever know. Sadly I am out for high meat, but will make some as soon as I find good meat that is not prefrozen.

### Glamazon — 2012-08-07T11:54:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/inger-will-you-donate-your-poo.2896/post-43390>

### Glamazon — 2012-08-08T04:42:16+0300

> **Cavemam;43700 wrote:** Glam - you are going to have to oversee any experiments!

I have done my fair share of enemas in my nursing career. That was back in the 80s when I graduated. Haven't done one in a looooong time. But DIY would save you a heck of a lotta money. Don't have to pay for the GI specialist consult. Probably have to drink mag citrate to clear out the bowel before. No procedure or room fee. etc. etc. etc. But no happy time IV meds either.

# Thread: Hack my GI Effects Test if you like
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-gi-effects-test-if-you-like.2897/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 23
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### vkiernan — 2012-08-07T03:19:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-gi-effects-test-if-you-like.2897/post-43336>

Had to do it as txt cuz the pdf was too big. Thanks much for any input.

Age: 49 Sex: Female

7/13/12

Report Date:

Date Received: 7/17/12

2100 Gastrointestinal Function Profile

Methodology: DNA Analysis, GC/MS, Microscopic,

Colorimetric, Automated Chemistry, ELISA

20% 40% 60% 80%

Results 1st 2nd 3rd 4th 5th

CFU/gram

95%

Reference

Range

Percentile Ranking by Quintile Consistency = Formed/Normal

Predominant Bacteria (E+007) E+007

Units and Reference Ranges

Organisms are detected by DNA analysis.

One colony forming unit (CFU) is equivalent to

one bacterium. Each genome detected

represents one cell, or one CFU. Results are

expressed in scientific notation, so an organism

reported as 2.5 E7 CFU/gram is read as 25

million colony forming units per gram of feces.

The cutoff for significance of Opportunistic

Bacteria has been set at 1.0E+ 005 (100,000).

These are levels above which clinically

significant growth may be present. Rather than

reporting semi-quantitative +1 to +4 levels, the

new methodology provides full quantitative

analysis.

Predominant Bacteria play major roles in

health. They provide colonization resistance

against potentially pathogenic organisms, aid in

digestion and absorption, produce vitamins and

SCFA's, and stimulate the GI immune system.

DNA probes allow detection of multiple species

(sp.) within a genus, so the genera that are

reported cover many species.

Obligate anaerobes

2.3 >= 1.3 1.6 6.7 Bacteroides sp. Â®

4.1 >= 1.0 1.5 6.2 Clostridia sp. Â®

2.2 >= 1.1 1.6 6.2 Prevotella sp. Â®

2.7 >= 1.1 1.6 7.4 Fusobacteria sp. Â®

2.1 >= 1.0 1.6 5.8 Streptomyces sp. Â®

5.7 >= 1.2 1.7 6.2 Mycoplasma sp. Â®

Facultative anaerobes

3.1 >= 1.2 1.8 7.8 Lactobacillus sp. Â®

4.1 >= 1.8 2.3 7.6 Bifidobacter sp. Â®

Obligate aerobes

5.3 >= 1.1 1.7 7.7 Escherichia coli (E. coli) Â®

Opportunistic Bacteria

No clinically significant amounts.

Opportunistic Bacteria may cause

symptoms and be associated with disease.

They can affect digestion and absorption,

nutrient production, pH and immune state.

Antibiotic sensitivity tests will be performed on

all opportunistic bacteria found, although

clinical history is usually considered to

determine treatment since the organisms are

not generally considered to be pathogens.

Page 1

Pathogenic Bacteria

95% Reference

Range

Helicobacter pylori to even low rates of

yeast growth. Potential symptoms include

diarrhea, headache, bloating, atopic dermatitis

and fatigue. Positives are reported as +1, +2,

+3 or +4 indicating >100, >1000, >10000 or

\>100000 pg DNA/g.

Parasites Expected Value

Parasite present; taxonomy unavailable. Positive Neg

A taxonomy unavailable finding likely indicates an ingested protozoan and not a human parasite. It

does not indicate treatment unless patient symptoms and other inflammatory markers are consistent

with parasite infection.

Parasites

Parasite infections are a major cause of

non-viral diarrhea. Symptoms may include

constipation, gas, bloating, increased allergy

response, colitis, nausea and distention.

Adiposity Index

Firmicutes 74 Â® = 20

The Adiposity Index is derived by using

DNA probes that detect multiple genera of the

phyla Firmicutes and Bacteroidetes.

Abnormalities of these phyla may be associated

with increased caloric extraction from food.

Drug Resistance Genes

aacA, aphD Neg gyrB, ParE Neg

mecA Neg PBP1a, 2B Neg

vanA, B, and C Neg

Drug Resistance Genes

aacA, aphD - Gentamycin, Kanamycin, and

Tobramycin

mecA - Methicillin

VanA, vanB, vanC - Vancomycin and Teicoplanin

GyrB, ParE - Ciprofoxacin and later quinolones

PBP1a, PBP2B - Penicillin

Page 2

2100 Gastrointestinal Function Profile

Methodology: DNA Analysis, GC/MS, Microscopic,

Colorimetric, Automated Chemistry, ELISA

Percentile Ranking by Quintile

1st 2nd 3rd 4th 5th

20% 40% 60% 80%

Results 95%

Reference Range

Beneficial SCFA

Beneficial SCFA

Short chain fatty acids (SCFA) are

produced by bacterial fermentation of dietary

polysaccharides and fiber. The product,

N-butyrate, is taken up and used to sustain the

normal activity of colonic epithielial cells.

Butyrate has been shown to lower the risk of

colitis and colorectal cancer. A healthy balance

of GI microbes depends on production of SCFA

by one specie to allow the normal growth of

another one in a complex cross-feeding

network.

Total SCFA 34 L >= 35 53Â® mM/g

n-Butyrate 5.3 >= 3.9 5.2Â® mM/g

Acetate % 65 47 - 77 71Â® % 52

Butyrate % 16 7 - 30 25Â® % 10

Propionate % 18 10 - 29 25Â® % 13

Valerate % 1.3 0.4 - 4.6 3.6 Â® % 1.0

Inflammation

Inflammation

Lactoferrin, an iron-binding glycoprotein,

is released in IBD but not in non-inflammatory

IBS. High levels are found in Crohn's, UC or

infection. WBC's are elevated in general

inflammation/infection. Mucus is often

visualized in acute GI inflammation.

Lactoferrin 2.3 Â® or other microbes. Low sIgA

can result from stress or malnutrition.

Anti-gliadin sIgA is a screening marker for

gluten sensitivity.

Fecal sIgA 25 5 - 161 144 Â® mg/dl 20

Anti-gliadin sIgA 7.1 H 100 ug/g 200

Triglycerides 100 Â®

Inflammation

Inflammation

Lactoferrin, an iron-binding glycoprotein,

is released in IBD but not in non-inflammatory

IBS. High levels are found in Crohn's, UC or

infection. WBC's are elevated in general

inflammation/infection. Mucus is often

visualized in acute GI inflammation.

Lactoferrin 2.3 Â® style="color: #FF0000;">High fecal sIgA indicates immune system

reactions to the presence of antigens from

bacteria, yeast or other microbes. Low sIgA

can result from stress or malnutrition.

Anti-gliadin sIgA is a screening marker for

gluten sensitivity.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-08-07T23:53:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-gi-effects-test-if-you-like.2897/post-43508>

I have the same fat malabsorption issue and my doc recommended I take ox bile. 500mg is too much so I dropped to 250mg and that with a regular enzyme seems to be just right! This is the one I use:[http://www.amazon.com/Ox-Bile-125-180-Capsules/dp/B003GHA5GS](http://http://www.amazon.com/Ox-Bile-125-180-Capsules/dp/B003GHA5GS) I found this to be a good one to play with small amounts and raise up if you need to.

### tweety — 2012-08-08T01:07:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-gi-effects-test-if-you-like.2897/post-43518>

Not to hijack, but Patty Cakes could you tell me the benefits you are experiencing from the ox bile? Do you also take HCL?

I also have fat malabsorption but I do take HCL. I wondered recently about ox bile but hesitate to add anything else.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-08-08T01:32:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-gi-effects-test-if-you-like.2897/post-43522>

Yes, Tweety I take both HCL and ox bile. I can't tell anything from just straight HCL. The HCL is for acid in the stomach to help digest protein. Then I added the ox bile at the high dose I got diarrhea so I knew something was working. But maybe a little too much! SO I went down to the 125mg size and started with one, then two and three (375mg). Sometimes when I eat a lot of fat I go more and sometimes I go less. I use my intuition on how much. So you may want to add just a small amount like the 125mg and see if you notice anything.

### tweety — 2012-08-08T02:59:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-gi-effects-test-if-you-like.2897/post-43542>

Ok, I will consider this in a couple of weeks. I have to go slower introducing more stuff bcs I tend to make a bunch of changes all at once, then never know why I am getting a particular reaction.

VKiernan- I know you have not been able to use HCL lately- whatever happened with your acid stomach symptoms? Have you used ox bile as well?

### vkiernan — 2012-08-08T04:54:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-gi-effects-test-if-you-like.2897/post-43568>

No ox bile as my gallbladder is working fine according to all the tests I had done last fall. I was under the impression that ox bile is for those with no gallbladder. Could totally be wrong on that. Sounds like I need to look up ox bile and get better informed.

As for the stomach acid stuff, had it thru the next day and then my stomach bloat went down and very little discomfort after that. Really have no idea why it came and why it left; just grateful it did. I'm guessing things are still changing from the seafood and from CT'ing. I have a messed up gut so I can only assume it will take some time.

As for HCL, I cannot take that stuff. Gives me acid reflux or at least a week or so it did. Too afraid to try it again. I really took a lot before and now I can't take any. Too me that is a good sign. I was eating those bad boys like candy before up to about 10 to 15 a day.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-08-08T05:54:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-gi-effects-test-if-you-like.2897/post-43593>

> **vkiernan;43878 wrote:** What was your number for the gluten issue? Do you have celiacs? I rarely eat any grains and don't know why my number is what it is. Maybe even rarely is too much. I might eat dairy a little in butter if we go out to eat but seldom grains unless I am getting it in something I am unaware of.

I have never been diagnosed as celiacs and only stopped gluten because of the Hashis. I have not had gluten knowingly for about 18 months and zero grains for 8 months. I'm extremely strict about the grain issue. I also don't eat dairy unless I get it eating out. We rarely eat out except for sushi and most of it is raw and no butter there. My doc thought I need to check my skin care products and shampoo for wheat or wheat derivatives for exposure. I don't know where else I could be getting it unless some crumbs are getting in my food from my step-sons bread!!

My number shocked me too! My Anti-gliadin was 7.4 marked as High. But I also showed Candida and h.pylori so my gut appears to have more issues that need to be resolved than yours.

### Destiny — 2012-08-08T13:44:12+0300

Patty, you need to eradicate that h. pylori. According to dr. K, it has to go even if you have to use antibiotics. I had two triple therapies (mixture of antibiotics and prilosec) and one quadruple one. The last one got rid of it.

### vkiernan — 2012-08-08T14:30:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-gi-effects-test-if-you-like.2897/post-43622>

Damn that you have hashis. As far as I know, I don't. I have been tested twice. Not sure thats enough tests or not. So your number for the Agi was the same as mine too. That is strange for sure. I did have Candida according to blood tests twice but have done a lot of things to try to rid myself of it. It said none so I am assuming but can't verify until I talk with Jack. Perhaps that is why my FT3 is rising??? I have also read though when the FT3 is over the range and your TSH is low that is due to the adrenals not working well. I know I need to do an ASI but that is supposed to give you results over a 6 month time frame I believe so that would not necessarily tell me if I have made progress in the short term would it? I am more confused all the time.

Now that I know you and I are both really in the same boat, I would like to compare more notes and what each of us is doing to help ourselves and the progress we are making. Might help us get there quicker. We are even the size basically. If you are game, pm me.

DH is doing testing too and will have a consult w/ Dr. K very soon! >
>

>
>

# Thread: My grandma's friend is recovering
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-grandmas-friend-is-recovering.2899/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jimmy82 — 2012-08-07T14:18:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-grandmas-friend-is-recovering.2899/post-43395>

As i have mentioned in my previous post my granny.... Who was recently diagnosed with dementia and we have shifted her to the old aged care center.... i must say that within a couple of months she has recovered really quickly and been asking the doctors and the care centers staff that when she should be able to go home... She can walk and do every thing she would like to the only reason we shifted her she was becoming aggressive day by day...

Last week when me and my friend arrive there to meet her the care center staff told us that she is really making it very hard for them ever since she had heard that they have discharged a patient right next to her.... We are all worried about her and are not finding a way what to do.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Howdy from Massachusetts!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/howdy-from-massachusetts.2901/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### rottzilla — 2012-08-07T16:36:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/howdy-from-massachusetts.2901/post-43415>

Howdy all, I'm relatively new. Just been reading and learning.

I lost about 50 pounds and kept it off for going on three years now, using hcg. I tried several rounds later to lose my last 35-40 lbs but just kept failing because of cheating. I guess I just either wasn't ready to finish it or I was just plain old tired of the diet. Anyway, now I'm 40 and trying to finish losing weight, but my hubby and I are trying to conceive, so I am trying to eat nutrient dense foods. I am low iron and worry about being nutritionally sound to host a baby, but at the same time, I desperately need to lose these pounds. I had a sciatic problem recently that scared the living heck out of me. It's been two months of long hard recovery and lots of pain.

Hoping to learn from all of you, and hoping you'll find time to help me. I'm desperate to lose the weight, but just can't seem to do it. (PS I've had lots of success before with Dr. Rosedale's diet so I'm familiar with leptin and using it to my advantage)

Anyway, nice meeting you all!

### Melinda — 2012-08-07T17:18:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/howdy-from-massachusetts.2901/post-43425>

Good luck on your journey. Not knowing much about your situation except that your fertility window is closing, I'd recommend you focus like a laser on "nutritionally sound to host a baby" and not worry about the weight. Actively losing weight is not a pg signal.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-08-07T20:11:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/howdy-from-massachusetts.2901/post-43469>

welcome to the road to optimal.

### caroline — 2012-08-08T04:32:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/howdy-from-massachusetts.2901/post-43561>

Howdy from down under and good luck on your journey - you have come o the right place!

### jimmight — 2012-08-10T08:37:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/howdy-from-massachusetts.2901/post-44085>

I take 10,000 IU of D3 three or four days a week, and 5,000 IU the rest.

Looking forward to learning from the community.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: BHRT experiences- pls share!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-experiences-pls-share.2903/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 205
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### tweety — 2012-08-07T17:16:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-experiences-pls-share.2903/post-43424>

So i have been on progesterone for nine months, from 15-30mg topical cream applications daily from day 15 to beginning of cycle. I also started keto DHEA a couple of months ago, and compounded T3 one month ago.

At my most recent testing, both saliva and blood levels show no increase in levels. In fact my progesterone and DHEA have dropped even lower. I asked a question to Dr. K, and he said now is the time for BHRT. But... I am on it and its not going anywhere. My cortisol is still low, so its not being shunted to cortisol either.

In addition to this, I am continuing to gain 1-2lbs each month with the progesterone application. The weight does not come off once I stop. It only increases when I start applying the cream again. My hormone doc is pulling the progesterone for now, and focusing on thyroid and adrenals. We also switched from straight T3 to dessicated thyroid.

Has anyone else experienced this?

Does anyone have research or ideas to help me think through this?

### [email redacted] — 2012-08-07T17:20:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-experiences-pls-share.2903/post-43427>

> **tweety;43715 wrote:** So i have been on progesterone for nine months, from 15-30mg topical cream applications daily from day 15 to beginning of cycle. I also started keto DHEA a couple of months ago, and compounded T3 one month ago.
>
>
>
> At my most recent testing, both saliva and blood levels show no increase in levels. In fact my progesterone and DHEA have dropped even lower. I asked a question to Dr. K, and he said now is the time for BHRT. But... I am on it and its not going anywhere. My cortisol is still low, so its not being shunted to cortisol either.
>
>
>
> In addition to this, I am continuing to gain 1-2lbs each month with the progesterone application. The weight does not come off once I stop. It only increases when I start applying the cream again. My hormone doc is pulling the progesterone for now, and focusing on thyroid and adrenals. We also switched from straight T3 to dessicated thyroid.
>
>
>
> Has anyone else experienced this?
>
> Does anyone have research or ideas to help me think through this?

I have nothing useful - I just wanted to share your misery, in case it loves company. It can be so defeating to work so hard at something and have "worse" results. You can figure this out. I spent some time answering your "ask jack" question before I realized it was an "ask jack". My advice was to ask Jack again as his answer was confusing. His answers are not always clear! Half the time I'm more confused after he answers - hehe perhaps it's his way of making us sign up for a consult faster! Pavlovian type training and all.

### Shijin13 — 2012-08-07T18:00:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-experiences-pls-share.2903/post-43434>

tweety

where are you applying the creams???

I changed location - on a recommendation from Colleen to the inner arm btwn elbow and armpit - and I'm begging to see a difference - we'll see where things stand when we re-test in September...

thre have been ppl who've noticed that w/not enough Pg - they gain weight. maybe you are one of those ppl who need oral BHRT Pg like prometrium (Jack thinks that's my case - and my PCP is leaning that way as well - especially if my Pg:E2 ratio doesn't come up this next round of testing)

not everyone absorbes the creams well...

as for the DHEA - are you taking it sublingulally? I break open my LEF DHEA and swish it around w/some water in my mouth until it disolves.

What brand of DHEA are you using? it could be what your taking your body isn't registering.

I was taking 100mg 7Keto Dhea and 100mg DHEA & 200mg of Pregnenolone - that cranked everything up - so my DHEA and Pregnenolone are now off the charts - and I've backed down to 15mg DHEA, 25mg 7Keto Dhea, and 25mg of Pregnenolone.

Also - you may want to consider trying a diffrent compounding pharmacy.

additionally you've got to fix the low cortisol, before anything else is going to really start taking notice. you might want to check out the thread on Why Factor X is actionable [http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?2194-So-you-want-to-know-why-FACTOR-X-matters-NOW](http://http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?2194-So-you-want-to-know-why-FACTOR-X-matters-NOW) - Jack talks about cycloset - there....it might be something you want to look into, and talk w/your doc about....

### Jack Kruse — 2012-08-07T19:45:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-experiences-pls-share.2903/post-43460>

or maybe your doctor is not aggressive enough with dosing........(my bet)

### colleencoble — 2012-08-07T21:19:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-experiences-pls-share.2903/post-43487>

I was about to say the same thing, Doc. WAY too low progesterone. Good grief, I take 150 mg a day of progesterone. Now you likely don't need as much as I do, but obviously you're not getting enough. I'd also bet you need pregnenolone.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-08-07T22:35:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-experiences-pls-share.2903/post-43498>

### [email redacted] — 2012-08-07T22:51:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-experiences-pls-share.2903/post-43501>

Can I share in the misery also? I have been taking 200mg progesterone(RDT) and as Colleen put it one time in regard to my numbers..."it's in the toilet". I just went to a new BHRT doc and should have info back on labs this week. I know you are ready for some progress and so am I

### Joann — 2012-08-07T22:56:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-experiences-pls-share.2903/post-43502>

Tweety, I can only relate my experience with BHRT.....I am coming from a different place since I didn't use hormones until after meno.....I have never had a rise in serum E, P, from using the creams or gels. Neither did a df (she uses P twice a day at 100mg dose) and no change or doesn't feel any different. otoh, I feel everything ...P goes to something, makes me feel nuts.

What about Testosterone?

When I first tried E, biest, I felt sad, foggy, not what I expected. my df felt nothing. I tweaked dose up and down, stopped. Then I asked for a stronger E, just estradiol, and had opposite response (stimulated, heart palps) Then went back to biest and low and behold ...my response was totally different. woke up the receptors? Last year I stopped using the biest for a months...started aging weight....2 lbs a week. went back on E and did not lose it. so I would say it definitely can wreak havoc on weight . when I first used E I lost 6 lbs without trying. since you are still cycling, means your hormones change throughout the month...I would think whatever hormones is in need of tweaking should be cycled if any.

I went to one of the "famous" docs, he gave me his Biest, I told him I couldn't handle the high dose, but I had to try his protocol. I was hot flashing, no sleep. He told me to put the cream on the soles of my feet. Major hot flashes, no sleep, worse than meno. He put me in his book of unusual responses and told my dh that his wife (me) had a unique body! LOL

Joann

### Glamazon — 2012-08-07T23:12:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-experiences-pls-share.2903/post-43503>

I am taking 700mg of DHEA daily. I started at 200mg. No adverse side affects but plenty of positive ones. Body recomp is going well although weight loss is low in comparison. I will keep adding 100mg a week until adverse side affects begins to show. I am dosing myself based on educational consult until I find a good BHRT doc. I found research that 2000mg have been shown to not exhibit adverse side effects. We are all individual. My baseline salivary hormone panel was nada...zombie zone...from zrtlabs. It was worse than someone who was 20 years older than myself and I am in my 50s.

### tweety — 2012-08-08T00:34:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-experiences-pls-share.2903/post-43511>

Wow! Thanks for all the great responses.

I thought my p was too low a dose, so I went to 30mg daily. I noticed an improvement in mood, but gained more weight. I suppose I could consider 45mg next month. And 60mg if necessary too.

Thing is, my E came down, so my ratio of P/E is now within range- 117. (range 45-455).

I am taking 50mg DHEA for the last week. At first I noticed great energy. Then nothing. Back to naps. And lately I have felt irritable, so I am backing off a bit. I will try sublingual next dose though.

I am removing keto DHEA for now. My drs next step will be to consider estrogen. I am hoping if I take the P out for a few months I can lose some of this weight, then I will experiment with it at a higher dose.

As far as thyroid, she's got me starting on 1 grain of compounded desiccated thyroid. She told me to give it 3 mos. I am going to give it a week, then I want to increase it to 2 grains. I have done some reading on that. Everything I read says staying at a low starting dose too long leads to more aggressive hypothyroid issues.

### tweety — 2012-08-08T00:36:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-experiences-pls-share.2903/post-43512>

Oh yeah- I have been applying the cream to my inner arms all along.

I don't seem to metabolize things well, so it seems odd to think I may do better on progesterone pills more than topical cream, but it's a consideration.

### tweety — 2012-08-08T00:37:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-experiences-pls-share.2903/post-43513>

Colleen- you take 150mg oral or topical progesterone?

I was told 30 is max for topical, but oral doses are around 100-200 bcs you absorb less.

And I just started on 50mg pregnenolone yesterday. I'll be watching to see what effect it has. I want to change my

Protocol, but I don't want to change so much that I don't know what is doing what!

### Shijin13 — 2012-08-08T00:54:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-experiences-pls-share.2903/post-43515>

FWIW Tweety while your Pg:E2 maybe in range its still in the bottom quartile - Jack want's to see women in the top Quartile - so you're going to want to be closer to 400 vs 100...

### Joann — 2012-08-08T01:20:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-experiences-pls-share.2903/post-43519>

The P cream usually has a percentage...like 5 % = 50 mg/gm so if you are using 3 % and using 1/4 tsp which is a gm then it should be 30 mg. a script can be made to whatever you want. otc I think is at most 3%

### Patty Cakes — 2012-08-08T01:28:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-experiences-pls-share.2903/post-43521>

Tweety, going from 1 grain thyroid med to 2 grains is a big jump all in one shot. I raised mine 1/2 grain each time (every 10 days to 2 weeks) until symptoms were gone. I'm worried that you may go hyper and you don't want that. Raising slowly, albeit not too slowly, you need to let your body adjust as you raise. Can you link me to the place that talks about raising a grain at a time is OK. Also, you may want to multi dose. One dose in the morning and one in the afternoon. It's easier on your body that way.

From Stopthethyroidmadness.com

GOING UP WITH DOSAGES WAY TOO FAST. This was observed a few years ago: a doctor put his patient on desiccated thyroid. One grain, then 2 grains, 3 grains, 4 grains, 5 grains, then 6 grains. But the problem was that he did this within 4-5 weeks! OUCH. He started to find himself majorly overdosed with symptoms to match (high heart rate, sweating). He had to stop for a few weeks… then resume again at one grain and do it the right way–approx. 1/2 grain raise every two weeks, slowing down in the 2-3 grain area (and for some, slowing down on even lower amounts).

>

> I am removing keto DHEA for now. My drs next step will be to consider estrogen. I am hoping if I take the P out for a few months I can lose some of this weight, then I will experiment with it at a higher dose.

> As far as thyroid, she's got me starting on 1 grain of compounded desiccated thyroid. She told me to give it 3 mos. I am going to give it a week, then I want to increase it to 2 grains. I have done some reading on that. Everything I read says staying at a low starting dose too long leads to more aggressive hypothyroid issues.

Regarding the DHEA response. My same experience at 200mg. Dr. K has said (paraphrasing) at first it is felt as it enters the system then sinks down to where it is needed most.

You gotta top of the tank.You are probably no where close to filling the tank. You just keep putting in squirts. That's is why I am keeping on adding until I have adverse symptoms.Then I will back off some. Having repeat labs done in September so that will be a good pic of where I am since I started supplementing the first of last month.

700 mg for me has been a watershed dose. At this dose my cravings have gone way down and my appetite has disappeared. My body comp is changing, my legs are leaning out and getting defined (and I am pretty heavy). I sleep better and deeper. I had no idea that hormone supplementation could do that.

I am post menopausal so your mileage may vary.

### freshveggies — 2012-08-08T23:27:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-experiences-pls-share.2903/post-43754>

I believe when you see progesterone cream mentioned with the Dzugan ladies---it is really a gel that is an oil/water emulsioni that also contains permeation enhancers. This is different from the creams that others typically get from their doctor that is sometimes in a versa base. There is a better absorption with this than cream.

I used the vaginal suppositories made with a capsule in olive oil base. I just never really felt good on them.

### kathylu — 2012-08-08T23:30:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-experiences-pls-share.2903/post-43755>

I use 5 % progesterone cream, 1 ml morning, 1 ml evening. That's 100 mg per day. My progesterone blood level is still quite low, although I have gone from undetectable to 1.4 ng/ml (early follicular level). I am switching to inner arm (per Colleeen's suggestion) from inner thighs for application...hopefully that will make a difference.

### tweety — 2012-08-09T01:31:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-experiences-pls-share.2903/post-43772>

> **kathylu;44046 wrote:** I use 5 % progesterone cream, 1 ml morning, 1 ml evening. That's 100 mg per day. My progesterone blood level is still quite low, although I have gone from undetectable to 1.4 ng/ml (early follicular level). I am switching to inner arm (per Colleeen's suggestion) from inner thighs for application...hopefully that will make a difference.

So for the ladies on a strong dose, all the docs and pharmacists around here are under the impression that 30mg is as high as one can go. Anything around 100mg would be considered insanely high!

For those of you whose dr's have prescribed the stronger cream and higher dose- what are your particulars if you don't mind sharing? I am 34 and not menopausal, and I think my age and situation are keeping drs from feeling comfortable with strong doses. Is menopause a factor in prescribing higher doses of progesterone?

I am gaining and gaining every month with the cream. I am supposed to start today, and even though I have not added the cream I have already jumped up in weight and bloating. It only happens around day 15, which is when the cream gets used. On one hand I want to try a higher dose and see if it helps, but I am a little (ok, a lot) afraid of gaining even higher amounts of weight.

I think my doc is open minded though. She prescribed DHEA for me, wanted me to start on 5mg for three mos. I mentioned I already started it and was on 50mg daily!  Her response was, ok if that works, go with it. She didn't admonish me, and was fine with my decision. So I am thinking when I decide what I want to do with progesterone she will go with me on it. I just don't know what it is I want yet!

### tweety — 2012-08-09T01:33:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-experiences-pls-share.2903/post-43773>

Thanks Colleen, by the way. I am applying it to my inner arms when I do apply.

PS All the info on the internet insists being on too high/strong a dose of progesterone makes you gain weight. Not if its too low a dose. I can't decide if this is more CW regarding BHRT or what. I am thinking it is...

It only happens around day 15, which is when the cream gets used. Her response was, ok if that works, go with it. She didn't admonish me, and was fine with my decision. So I am thinking when I decide what I want to do with progesterone she will go with me on it. You said: I am gaining and gaining every month with the cream. I am supposed to start today, and even though I have not added the cream I have already jumped up in weight and bloating. It

I would take this as you are already estrogen dominant that is why you are gaining and bloated. That is exactly what happens to me. That is why I feel I need progesterone all month long. Did you post your estrogen and progesterone labs? Would like to see them.

By then it is hard to overcome the estrogen dominance from the first half of they cycle if you wait until day 15 to add in progesterone. I went through this too. So I know how you feel. I would litterly go up a whole pant size.

### Shijin13 — 2012-08-09T14:44:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-experiences-pls-share.2903/post-43856>

I saw weight loss as soon as I started supplementing Pg.

I'm using the 5% Pg in a cyclical fashion

.4ml Pg during TOTM

.8 ml Pg for 10 days + .1ml TriEst

1.0ml Pg for the remainder of my cycle

I still need more as in still having migraines (I've had 1 last Sat). We'll see what my Pg:E2 ratio is in September when we test again. I think I need more Pg on the back end. Fortunately my DHEA & Pregenolone are off the charts do the support is there

This plus the cycloset & epipaleo are beginning to make a difference for me. I still have a long way to go. But I'm on my way. Each day is fwd progress.

I think it will be interesting what my MW says when I see her for my annual.

### Glamazon — 2012-08-09T19:26:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-experiences-pls-share.2903/post-43918>

> **freshveggies;44189 wrote:** I have been chasing horomones for years. The last 5 years, I had a doc that would let me dose myself and use what I wanted. I still couldn't get it figured out. There is a science and an art behind it. I also have tried 2 anti aging docs and another well known hormone expert to no avail. I am hoping Dzugan can get the job done right for me.

I didn't realize hormones were so important until I saw my panel this year. I consider it just one leg of chair for optimal. The others are nutrition, gut health and CT. If any of these are not optimal there will always be wobbliness and uncertainty. The fact that I am working on getting my hormones optimal has made significant changes in my health and well being and supports the other legs of the chair. I was definately a prisoner of CW and had no idea why I would see no results. I worked on CW nutrition and exercise for years always falling quickly because unknown to me I was fighting a bad gut and poor hormones which affected my mind and attitude/motivation/energy as well as my body. Hopefully, as you become more optimal in the other legs you will see more stabilization of the hormone leg. I have to keep reviewing the blog and the quilt to see this is not just a multifactoral approach but a multidimensional one. I am certainly not at optimal but I definately have much more hope than I have ever had in the last 40 years that I could become so.

### Glamazon — 2012-08-09T19:31:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-experiences-pls-share.2903/post-43919>

I just wanted to add we need to be kinder to ourselves. Dr. K has been on this for seven years. Some of us "senior" female members have just been following the epi paleo plan since the beginning of the year. I had to relax about results.  I have had great results over time but they are mostly internal and feeling states of being. Not so much on the exterior yet although that is slowly improving.

### tweety — 2012-08-11T14:27:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-experiences-pls-share.2903/post-44294>

> **freshveggies;44558 wrote:** question. ----I know it can be private, but I need some help. I sometimes get a burning sensation down there and last time, it went away after I layed down for bed and had put on my P. I am thinking it is a imbalance of P/E. Does anyone know what is too high or too low? I am on cd 19. I was thinking E was dipping too low now that I upped my P, but not sure.

I do. Right before my period starts I get more burning sensations, and sometimes feeling like I have a yeast infection. Then it clears up once I start. I thought mine was connected to candida, but apparently not. What my GP told me was that as women head to menopause, and their progesterone drops, they have higher levels of yeast but they are not even aware. This sounds in line with what we have been learning from Dr. K, but not sure if its accurate or not. And i have not had too low E that I'm aware of.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-experiences-pls-share.2903/post-45021>

> **Jack Kruse;45347 wrote:** he does it because cortisol is highest in AM and this means progesterone should be lowest in AM.......naturally he is trying to upset the natural state where I think we should go the other way........

Interesting - I've always done my dosing at night - and only recently have split it up btwn am/pm...with the lower dose in the am. I've done this intuitively... Dzugan pretty much lets my intuition, my PCP's opinion and labs guide where we go w/supplementation. Though the last update, he held back on the dosing I wanted to move up to.... we'll see what labs say in September.... PCP is looking at both blood work and urine hormone panels to ensure we've got the whole picture.

### karicat — 2012-08-15T18:27:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-experiences-pls-share.2903/post-45034>

I'm on Dzugan's program too and it is correct that he gives the highest progesterone dose in the morning, I always wondered why. That's why the other day I was asking Gretchen how she was taking hers as she's with Dzugan too. And it usually makes me sleepy, progesterone always does that to me.

Interesting what Jack says about this related to cortisol, as I've just received my saliva test results (cortisol) and morning is somehow low and noon is definitely low. And I've been having a very hard time waking up and staying up, usually I take a short nap around 2.00 pm.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-08-15T18:32:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-experiences-pls-share.2903/post-45035>

### karicat — 2012-08-15T19:11:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-experiences-pls-share.2903/post-45042>

Thanks Jack, I will.

They have already reduced my progesterone dose a lot (from the amount we used as a start, I had almost no progest), because around the third month of my program, I started having (and still do unfortunately, but at least milder and less frequent), what I call anxiety episodes, but I don't know what they are as they are new to me.

I feel something on my chest, like a pressure, feel hot, sometimes I've seen my heart rate go up to 90/95 bpm, my arms weak, but the most distinct thing for me is like an internal vibration on my head and upper part of my body, a buzzy feeling, very weird and very uncomfortable. At one time I was getting them at noon, evening, and night !! now, that is much much better, but I'm still waking up sometimes in the middle of the night with all these symptoms and it takes me a while to get back to sleep. And they are more physical than psychological, that's why I don't know if it is correct to call them anxiety attacks.

But I'll definitely discuss with them changing the progesterone timing. Thank you !!

### LinD — 2012-08-16T00:37:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-experiences-pls-share.2903/post-45086>

So excited! I MIGHT have found a possible doctor. I was thinking of someone I know that might have a helpful tip and I was right. She wrote:

"Hi, just returned for my workout and have some spare time so I thought I'd give you what limited info I have regarding Docs. But first let me say, you look incredible these days!!

About 5yrs ago I began having several bizarre health problems so I sought out a well renowned physician at the Institute of Endocrinology and Preventive Medicine in Los Colinas. Her name is Dr. Marina Johnson. She performs extensive medical testing and the most detailed background history I've ever been though. She literally cured my IBS problems that I'd had all my life, discovered my low thyroid and a couple of autoimmune problems. She is the one who got me eating a paleo based diet and she believes in finding the root cause of problems as opposed to just throwing meds at them. I can't say enough good things about her.

2 yrs ago I switched to the FEM Center. They have 2 locations, I go to the one here in Ft Worth, my sister goes to the one in Colleyville. I like this one, she is not thrilled with the one she goes to because she says it's more OB/Gyn as opposed to wellness.

The only reason I switched is because Dr Johnson does not accept any type of insurance. You have to pay upfront and then file your own. I had UHC at the time and did manage to get approval for the visits to be paid as in network. However, they would always pay wrong and I had to constantly appeal (sometimes 2-3 times) to get the claims paid. I also had to call them every so often to get more visits approved which was an ordeal. got to be too much hassle and she is NOT cheap. I remember the 1st visit was around $5000 and you have to pay at the time of service. I hated leaving but I had girlfriends that spoke highly of the FEM Centre so I decided to try.

Fem Center - I see Dr Dan but I don't use him as my primary care physician. I see another Dr for illnesses. Dr Dan is good with wellness stuff. He monitors my hormones (I had to have a total hysterectomy back in 1986), I'm on bioidentical hormone replacement therapy. he also monitors my thyroid (I also have a low thyroid and take a compounded medicine), and he monitors and checks my vitamin levels and adrenal levels ect. The FEM center has specific docs and nutritionist that are paleo but I don't really get into nutrition with them. I feel like I read and study enough about that on my own.

I continue to struggle with autoimmune problems but I definitely feel better than I did 5-6 yrs ago. most recently I had the CSF leak repair which set me back a bit. when you can do nothing but lay around for several months it's easy to start eating garbage again, but I am now back on track.

I hope this helped some, keep me posted and I love reading your health posts! good luck with the new house, we just recently put ours on the market. my hubby is getting ready to retire and we're building out at PK lake. can't wait!

Call me anytime."

Sounds promising! While I cannot do the $5000 doc, I can check out the FEM Center. If I could afford the $5K doctor, I would go see Dr K in a heartbeat.

I did share with her this site and the blog. ;-)

### karicat — 2012-08-16T16:36:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-experiences-pls-share.2903/post-45194>

Hi Gretchen, and thanks for your comments.

There is definitely something weird going on with my situation as these "anxiety" issues have diminished, but definitely not disappeared (I'm getting them every night though milder than before).

A couple of days ago I got back my cortisol saliva test results which I'll send to dr Dzugan today. They don't look terrible, but don't look very good also. And besides, I'm no expert and I need someone to take a look at them within the context of my whole situation. I hope Dr Kruse will be able to help me when I have my consult with him.

I also had some labs done in Peru a couple of weeks ago, and based on dose and my huge anxiety issues, I'm off the 7 keto dhea, increased nature throid (1 grain now), lowered progesterone, and almost no triest.

Next time I travel to the US in October I'll get Dzugan's complete lab panel again (couldn't do the whole thing here, it was even more expensive than the us, and they didn't have everything that is needed) and some of the tests Jack has recommended as I can't find them here in Peru.

My thyroid levels are still low, my p:e ratio still low (but getting better, when I began my program, my total estrogens were 4417 pg/ml !! and progesterone 0.4 ng/ml). But I think Dr Dzugan decreased my progesterone in order to help me with the anxiety problem.

So, it has been a very bumpy road for me, but nevertheless, I'm feeling better than before starting my program with Dr Dzugan. So I'm quite optimist that within the right time and the right help (thanks Dr Kruse for all the info), I will get where I should be.

### karicat — 2012-08-16T16:46:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-experiences-pls-share.2903/post-45196>

Seahorse, I'm not sure about experimenting with the progesterone on your own. I've had many issues with my bhrt and if I would have been doing it on my own, it would have been even worse as it can be quite confusing for someone who doesn't have the experience and knowledge required. And if you will see a doctor in one month, maybe it would make sense to wait a little longer to get help. I definitely know the feeling of wanting to do something about our issues and feel better as soon as possible, but sometimes it is better to wait in order to do it right.

But, perhaps your body is not as sensitive as mine, and I'm taking a lot more things than just progesterone.

Good luck

### differentstory — 2012-08-20T20:58:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-experiences-pls-share.2903/post-45896>

Just wanted to say that I was doing the P, E, and T on the inner arms and after a month, it seemed to stop working. So I tried it vaginally and got a very different result. It is now the only way I will use it. Someone on this forum suggested it and had a link to a doctor that said it should be the only way to apply it. The article mentioned something about how applying it to the same muscle area over and over will just saturates(?) the muscle and it will no longer enter the blood stream - that is all from recollection and my recollection isn't as good these days!! Maybe someone else has a link to the doc's website? Anyways, for anyone struggling, go to Walgreens and buy a syringe and trying applying it vaginally a few times and see for yourself. I could see a total different after the second day.

I'm confused if you are talking cortisol or sex hormones. I thought I saw somewhere on here a while ago that Dr. K recommended (someone please correct me if I am wrong) a 30 day saliva sex hormone panel for cycling females. If you are menopausal then blood is better for sex hormones. The 30 day test gives you readings of where the hormones vary throughout the month which I would think would be valuable. But I know the ASI is the best test for adrenals. Blood not as good on that one.

### Destiny — 2012-08-22T13:24:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-experiences-pls-share.2903/post-46256>

I just ordered The DZugan Principle: Your Blood Doesn't Lie! I hope it is good.

Book Description

Publication Date: December 1, 2009

This book is the first of its kind in that it unifies the philosophy of what is loosely called anti-aging medicine, alternative medicine, holistic medicine and bio-identical hormone medicine under one principle. This principle states that many diseases are merely different manifestations of imbalances within blood and body chemistry. This book is not anti-drug per se, but it does strongly argue that the first step in achieving true health is the optimization of body chemistry through bio-identical hormones, vitamins, and other nutritional and natural supplements. When that optimization is achieved, the list of symptoms that improve is staggering.

This paradigm has allowed Dzugan Method physicians to effectively treat such wide-ranging conditions as high cholesterol/heart disease, menopause, ED, depression, chronic fatigue, fibromyalgia, migraine headaches, mental decline and macular degeneration.

### kathylu — 2012-08-22T13:38:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-experiences-pls-share.2903/post-46259>

Ladies, I think we need a lot more information before trying the Wiley protocol. I have been checking it out myself and I am a bit concerned. First the dosages are fabulously high and the incidence of side effects is quite large. Also, I've started to "check out" Wiley...I was a bit concerned when I realized she has no initials behind her name. It turns out that she has no credentials whatsoever. She's not an M.D., in fact it doesn't look like she has a bachelor's degree either. Now that doesn't mean that her ideas have no merit, but there hasn't been any testing of her protocol, either in animals or humans. I find that a concern. I think that Dzugan looks like a safer bet.

### vkiernan — 2012-08-22T14:36:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-experiences-pls-share.2903/post-46266>

> **kathylu;46647 wrote:** Ladies, I think we need a lot more information before trying the Wiley protocol. I have been checking it out myself and I am a bit concerned. First the dosages are fabulously high and the incidence of side effects is quite large. Also, I've started to "check out" Wiley...I was a bit concerned when I realized she has no initials behind her name. It turns out that she has no credentials whatsoever. She's not an M.D., in fact it doesn't look like she has a bachelor's degree either. Now that doesn't mean that her ideas have no merit, but there hasn't been any testing of her protocol, either in animals or humans. I find that a concern. I think that Dzugan looks like a safer bet.

You are correct on her not being an MD. She did write the book with Julie Taguchi, M.D. and Bent Formby, Ph.D. You have to be careful though it is under a specific name and there are others out there like hers. She is TS Wiley. Her dosing is based off of the natural rhythm that your body undergoes. It is not static dosing for 14 or 30 days a month. It is a bit more work but sounds like you are better off. I got the book 2nd hand for pretty cheap. I guess we all have to do what we feel is best. Darleen traveled 400 miles to go to one of these docs that follow her protocol. She swears by it. She does talk just a little about tri-est and how she thinks that confuses the receptors. Anyway, a lot of info is in the book and she is big into history (well, she is an anthropologist and cultural theorist).

### Patty Cakes — 2012-08-22T18:57:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-experiences-pls-share.2903/post-46350>

> **kathylu;46648 wrote:** Is anyone on the forum on the Wiley protocol and having good results? Please share.

I read of one person, from another forum, who felt the dosages were too high! Of course she made this statement AFTER she had done the protocol and apparently from her commentary had major problems due to the higher than normal dosages. I believe with any doc we need to be aware and do our own research. It may work for some and not others. As with anyone who uses something and it works great for them and their particular circumstances, it does not automatically correlate into the same outcome for another.

I wanted to read the book from a knowledge standpoint to possibly gain more insight into hormones. I'm currently enjoying Bombshell by Suzanne Somers and her stuff is really revolutionary. Am I going to go out and jump on them? Probably not with the ones I've read about so far but we can see how the science and study of how to help our broken bodies is continually advancing.

+1 Patty. After thinking about the dosing regime, since it stays within you for so long, does dosing up and down like she does it really need to happen that often? If it is taken by the body when it needs it, then why would we need to dose like that? Confusing to me for sure.

### SeaHorse — 2012-08-22T23:23:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-experiences-pls-share.2903/post-46411>

It seems to me that there is a dividing line in BHRT protocols with one side dosing to point where you are having a period (if they have stopped) again and the other side saying this is not a good idea. I'm still not convinced that having a period when you are menopausal is right, but I'm willing to listen. Clearly the individual context must be understood too...ie. migraine sufferers would take what works to keep their migraines away...etc.etc.
