### Shijin13 — 2012-08-23T01:20:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-experiences-pls-share.2903/post-46430>

Dzugan has my cycling my PG and Triest through out the month... pg is lower during TOTM, then increases for 10days along w/adding .1ml of Triest, then I increase again and drop the triest....

I'm seeing success - but I still have migraines, and the last 5days before I cycle my sleep goes to crap, so I think I need more pg the last 5days of my cycle...

so here's what my BHRT Supplementation looks like

PG .4 ml for TOTM

PG.8 ml & .1ml Triest for 10days

PG 1.0 ml for the remainder of my cycle

it looks like my cycle is adjusting to a 28 day cycle... but I guess we'll see....

### Joann — 2012-08-23T01:22:06+0300

Clearly the individual context must be understood too...ie. migraine sufferers would take what works to keep their migraines away...etc.etc.

When I firsr heard of cycling after meno I thought it was a good idea. maybe think I am younger. lol but then I started thinking about cells aging and the building of a lining and sloughing off every month.....more chance for "mistake"?

Uzzi reis thinks its nuts.

fwiw When I was peri I started having something called "scintillating scotoma" ocular migraines? scared the \*\*\*\* out of me. thought I was stroking. doc I had then said to use borage seed oil, I did and they stopped. when I finished with cycling, post meno never had it happen BUT when I started using E for uti's I was getting all the time, they came back. That was an eye opener. So in my case I think the fluctuations of E cause this "silent migraine".

### Shijin13 — 2012-08-23T01:35:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-experiences-pls-share.2903/post-46440>

I'm on 5% Formula 1 gel 50mg

I split my dose btwn am/pm

so TOTM PG .4ml is split btwn am/pm

next 10day .8ml is split btwn am/pm

remainder of cycle is 1.0 ml split .4ml am/.6ml pm

I forget the calculations to do the conversions... I think I did them on the other BHRT Thread.. [http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?1682-BHRT-Advice-suggestions-and](http://http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?1682-BHRT-Advice-suggestions-and)

Yeah the migraines are mostly gone - though today I feel like I've got one lurking...add that to the bad sleep the past couple of nights - all leads me to the conclusion I need more PG at the end of my cycle, for the last 5 days... I feel off right now so I know TOTM is soon.

Jack suggested in my consults considering 200mg prometrium and my PCP wants to see my labs and see what adjustments Dzugan does

one things for sure my thyroid is going crazy... I'm constantly hot, and can't cool off unless the AC is cranked or I'm in a pool of ice... I think its time to push for some Cytomel again...

### Shijin13 — 2012-08-23T04:03:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-experiences-pls-share.2903/post-46466>

> **Joann;46851 wrote:** Shin Feeling hot would not make me think more thyroid maybe too much? just a thought

I used to be always cold. And my TSH was "normal" Now that my thyroid had turned on when I start getting really hot usually my TSH is sky high. The question is what does my FT3:RT3 ratio look like

This was how I was all last winter and my TSH was out of bounds & my T3 was non existent

### Joann — 2012-08-23T04:24:36+0300

Interesting. guess like dr K says: Context. when I was peri I was hot all the time...use to stick my head in freezer, the ac was set at 65, then feel faint outside in the heat.

when I first started using E sometimes I would get cold or more precisely, everyone was hot from weather and I didnt notice. I would lower dose of E....thinking it was scewing with my thyroid. getting a SHBG test to make sure it isn't high, since E and Thyroid are tied and shbg will go after E when raised and Thyroid hormone.

so much to put together

Yeah well I'm extremely E dominant even after 3 mths of BHRT & T dominant. That's the joys of having PCOS & migraines trying to fix 2 things.

When we tested at the end of may my Pg:E2 ratio wasn't even in range it was 75:1, prior to that it was 8:1 & 4:1. Yeah that low on Pg. I really don't think I need the TriEst going off my intuition but I guess we'll see w/the next set of labs in 3wks

### Shijin13 — 2012-08-23T15:49:17+0300

> **vkiernan;46920 wrote:** Gretch, with the pee thing. I have it too. What I noticed is when I supplemented with T, it went away. You probably are though with your protocol.

hahaha... no T for me. my T production is above range w/o DHEA... now its really out of range... LOL... so its not that...I will provide a comment about this to Dzugan for my next update. I'm looking fwd to when my Pg:E2 is at the top of the range - b/c once that happens - My T will start to work its magic on Body comp, then add that to the CT... OH YEAH

### [email redacted] — 2012-08-25T17:17:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-experiences-pls-share.2903/post-47015>

> **Joann;47204 wrote:** I'm having one right now. haven't been feeling well for a few days, when I stand I feel like bp is low. hypoglycemia, hypotension? maybe connected

I often get them at work mid morning, real PITA because I can\`t read or do anything while it happens. lasts about 20-25 minutes all in.

I\`ve tried NSAIDs etc and nothing touched them, the only thing that shortens the process (has never stopped it tho) has been to eat something as soon as it happens.

if you google occular migraine and hypoglycemia there\`s quite a bit out there on it. fwiw these have more or less disappeared by doing LR, as long as I get the adequate protein and fat in for me they are few and far between

Yeah, reading is out, looking at peeps faces is tho weird lol if I put sunglasses on it diminishes the effect.

When this first occurred I looked in Tabor's med dictionary ...all it stated was Fatigue, stress, and it being a blind spot. no one knew had them. Then a friend called me from her work to tell me she was having one while looking at computer screen. Then my hubby had one at work looking at his computer screen. They were both thankful that I mentioned it first or they would have freaked. lol It seems it is common but maybe not "shared".

The borage seed oil (GLA) something to do with Prostaglandins....pg1, pg2 and inflammation is where my doc back then was going for. in the 90's he was cool doc, he passed on....he was interested. don't have one like that now

### [email redacted] — 2012-08-26T17:01:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-experiences-pls-share.2903/post-47232>

> **Joann;47568 wrote:** Yeah, reading is out, looking at peeps faces is tho weird lol if I put sunglasses on it diminishes the effect.
>
> When this first occurred I looked in Tabor's med dictionary ...all it stated was Fatigue, stress, and it being a blind spot. no one knew had them. Then a friend called me from her work to tell me she was having one while looking at computer screen. Then my hubby had one at work looking at his computer screen. They were both thankful that I mentioned it first or they would have freaked. lol It seems it is common but maybe not "shared".
>
> The borage seed oil (GLA) something to do with Prostaglandins....pg1, pg2 and inflammation is where my doc back then was going for. in the 90's he was cool doc, he passed on....he was interested. don't have one like that now

I was lucky my GP knew what they were so no total freak out but wasn\`t as good as yours cause no solution offered.

if they come back I\`ll try the borage seed oil but I\`m hoping (fingers crossed) that the 3 meals no snacking keeps the bs on a relatively even keel and keeps these babies at bay.

Interesting connection about flourescents and computer screens, would almost call it a home run connection except I\`ve been known to get them while driving as well. I\`m sure though the lights and comps aren\`t helping!

### natural_girl — 2012-08-26T17:55:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-experiences-pls-share.2903/post-47254>

Ok ladies I need your thoughts, suggestions, experiences. I did update Dzugan but have not heard back yet. So I am on P 3 weeks of the month & he effectively doubled my dosage 11 days ago. I was also before the increase only taking at night but with the increase am now taking morning & evening.

So after a few days of the increase I noticed I was starting to hold more water & feel much heavier (afraid to get on the scale!), then GI upset -indigestion & heartburn, some breakthrough spotting & acne break outs on my face.

I also want to mention today is day 24 of my cycle which have been 26 days lately since starting P before that they were 28 days.

After about 5 days the GI stuff went away and some of the bloating but I still feel & look bloated. Today I woke up with a rash on my stomach. The same rash I got when I began CT'ing?!?! This hormone stuff really is crazy. I won't get new labs drawn until mid-September to know where my P/E ratio is. Anyone experience this?

### Melinda — 2012-08-27T00:03:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-experiences-pls-share.2903/post-47357>

> **Cavemam;47750 wrote:** Any suggestions on where Husband should put this? (aside from trash) I am now hoping my testosterone is low so I can use it!

Not an authority about this but don't throw it away. Just use it up and then switch to the injections. I don't think it is as effective but it would be a start. The insert should suggest a couple of places to put it, then cover with clothing and much later wash it off before snuggling anyone.

### Lyndra — 2012-08-27T01:30:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-experiences-pls-share.2903/post-47372>

I have a couple of hormone books with some info that may or may not help since the info is targeted towards women.

Uzzi Reiss, MD ob/gyn, in Natural Superwoman says he prefers cream or gel over injections because it's easier to tweak the dose and he says the effects of injections last for weeks & months. For women, he suggests applying to the soles of the feet to prevent unwanted hair growth.

In Natural Hormone Balance, Uzzi Reiss tells women to apply it topically:

1) inner thigh to benefit the whole body, including bones, cardiovascular system, and the emotions

2) perineum, to distribute testosterone throughout the pelvic cavity to strengthen & restore the bladder muscle, the sexual organs, and the muscles above

3) small labia to target the vagina more specifically

### Destiny — 2012-08-27T14:25:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-experiences-pls-share.2903/post-47462>

When I stared supplementing with PG, I felt no good for weeks on a smallest amount. Testing makes this more bearable when you know you are deficient! I think if you were deficient for a number of years, your body might have a strong reaction to a sudden constant supply.

Now, I am able to take much more without feeling off.

### Shijin13 — 2012-08-27T14:44:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-experiences-pls-share.2903/post-47464>

Ok since switching application sites this last cycle wasn't as hellish. Last Thursday night I was a bit short tempered - I could definitely tell TOTM was near. I also had a headache but it wasn't a migraine. I had slight spotting thrus night right before bed but not enough to call it TOTM. Which officially started on friday. I was super crampy/achy all day friday & just plain Slugish on Sat By Sunday it was barely a trickle 3 day TOTM. Count me in especially since it was super light

I do know that I do need more Pg at the end of my cycle b/c my sleep goes to \*\*\*\* about 5 days before starting not even orgasam before bed helps w/sleep. So I know I need to go up to at least 1.2ml the last 5 days of cycle

Hoping my Pg:E2 ratio gets into range this round of testing

### vkiernan — 2012-08-27T20:17:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-experiences-pls-share.2903/post-47509>

> **natural\_girl;47748 wrote:** Gretchen,
>
>
>
> Why more Pg when it is causing all of these side effects?

I have read that when you are e-dom. you have to take a LOT to overcome the estrogen. Iirc, it was over 200 mg a day. I don't claim to fully understand it. Something about not enough actually pushes the estrogen higher hence the bloating, etc. Start googling progesterone dosing. There is a lot out there. Taking it now is a lot different than last fall but my estrogen is only 11% of what it use to be to. My ratio still sucks but I have to give it time. I have noticed that it makes me pee a lot all day. Not at night though. At least I believe that is what it is from. It is also very cool in my office so might be a combo but I wasn't peeing like this before.

### fitness@home — 2012-08-28T01:25:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-experiences-pls-share.2903/post-47581>

Wanted to share my BHRT experience and tell those that are frustrated by their current doctor to not give up. If you arenâ€™t happy with the care you are receiving then it is time to move on.

This was my situation. The OBGYN I saw in June ran every test I wanted and it was an extensive list; and then suggested some additional testing. Unfortunately, she did not want to (or was unable to) explain the results of the more complicated testing. She forwarded the data to a head pharmacist at a compounding pharmacy for recommendations.

After a frustrating month of calls and emails with the pharmacy I learned the delays were caused by a lack of response from the doctorâ€™s office. Time for me to look for another doctorâ€¦I never did get a new BHRT prescription from her.

I saw a post on this forum from another member who felt she had found a very good doctor in our area. I PMâ€™d her and she was gracious enough to share the doctorâ€™s name (thanks PrimalPam!). I made an appointment and sent in my medical history, all test results, and a copy of my education consult for her to listen to.

My first appt was today. I am very impressed with their office staff and especially the doctor. I first met with the nutritionist. They recommend Paleo which lines up nicely with my current WOE. We also discussed current meds & supplements.

Then I saw the doctor - who specializes in menopause management. She had taken the time to review **ALL** the data I had provided and also listened to my educational consult _twice_ over the weekend. There were notes written all over the materials I had sent.

We spent an hour going over everything and she made her recommendations. Three prescriptions have been called in to my compounding pharmacy. The estrogen/testosterone will be in cream form and the progesterone will be taken orally (my progesterone was upped to 200 mg daily). The thyroid meds will be compounded â€“ no added fillers.

I also had some additional blood work this morning and will go back in late next week for the Genova Diagnostics NutrEval and the Sprectracell LPP (Lipoprotein Particle Profile) testing. My follow up appt for those test results is already scheduled.

Finally! My gut tells me I am moving in the right direction.

The moral of this story is Don't Give Up...

### vkiernan — 2012-08-28T20:28:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-experiences-pls-share.2903/post-47833>

> **Shijin13;48206 wrote:** Yes it can help what dose are you on during TOTM? Try adding .2ml to your regular dose and see if that helps

Self-dosing for right now. Got to figure out yet when I need it and when I don't. Last month I stopped too soon on day 1 and shouldn't have. I needed it for a carryover to smooth things out. I also found out last month that I think I started it too early. It made me kind of a cry baby. Didn't like that at all. I have to wait for my next appt. before she starts me cycling. I am only taking 20 mg 2x day right now. My breast tenderness went away very fast this month and actually it wasn't breast so much as nipple. It use to be all breast and was a killer. Now, not so bad.

On the down side, the pharmacy gave me the stupid click thing this time. Next time I will ask for the syringe. Much more accurate and I can up or down the dose a whole lot easier. Still thinking about TS Wiley or Dhugan but I have to see what she is going to do first and give her a chance.

### Destiny — 2012-09-09T14:04:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-experiences-pls-share.2903/post-50457>

I increased my progesterone supplementation and started a small dose of DHEA. My FBG went up and is around 110 or higher. Before it was not great either but it was in 90's.

Did any of you experienced this? Any suggestions?

### kathylu — 2012-09-09T14:30:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-experiences-pls-share.2903/post-50459>

>
>
>
> Did any of you experienced this? Any suggestions?

Destiny, that's interesting. I hadn't put the two together, but I started bhrt about 4 months ago. My FBG was around 105 before that and now it's around 120. Could be coincidence, but it makes me wonder. I just thought my FBG was getting worse b/c of my sleep apnea. I'll do more research into the topic when I have some time.

### freshveggies — 2012-09-11T01:22:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-experiences-pls-share.2903/post-50839>

HI Dzugan ladies, I am on the program and have a question. What is their philosophy about only using .1ml of progesterone at ngiht. I need more. Don't we use all of it up every 12 hours. I called my advisor and said I want more explanations about what they changed and why --- now they sent another update to my doctor to just explain to me what they changed. It was not even what I asked them for. I wish we could communicate better. I would love to ask a question, but doesn't seem like they can answer any without going through my doctor.

Another question. my program says .1ml estrogen at night and .1ml progesterone at night. Do you think this would be a wise idea to try. During the day they have me on .1ml estrogen and .3ml progesterone. I would think at night that wouldn't be enough progesterone to balance the estrogen. I have not been using any estrogen at night and I have been using more progesterone at night. I am a little afraid to try this.

my progesterone is 100mg per ml and my estrogen is triest at 1.25mg per ml.

### Shijin13 — 2012-09-17T04:23:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-experiences-pls-share.2903/post-52050>

Ughhh

Hormone days from hell this wkend. Yesterday woke up at 4:30 w/what started to be a migraine dh too havoc to keep him from bothering me. And I warded the damn thing off

Then today around 10am BAM! (emeril style) I get bulldozed be a wicked ass migraine one of the worst I've had since starting BHRT Had to breakdown and take the Target version of exerting migraine (2xs no less it was that bad). Now my stomach is rioting (could be the leftover fish from Friday though). Ugh

So I look at what day it is in my cycle yup cd24 hormone drop. Yeah!!! Couple that w/massive acne. I'm still no where I should be regarding Pg:E2 ratio

Seriously going to be pushing for increasing my dosages. Going to ask to move up big time. I need more Pg. looking back I can't believe I survived w/these 3-4c/month.

### mafixit — 2012-09-18T00:25:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-experiences-pls-share.2903/post-52225>

> **Joann;46830 wrote:** fwiw When I was peri I started having something called "scintillating scotoma" ocular migraines? scared the \*\*\*\* out of me. thought I was stroking. doc I had then said to use borage seed oil, I did and they stopped. when I finished with cycling, post meno never had it happen BUT when I started using E for uti's I was getting all the time, they came back. That was an eye opener. So in my case I think the fluctuations of E cause this "silent migraine".

I have had 3 ocular migraines in the past 2 years, first one in 2010. First one scared the crap out of me - thought I was having another retinal detachment. Second one was annoying, third one my attitude was 'really, another one?' and I cleaned the bathroom until it was over, since I couldn't see my computer screen while it was happening so I could not work.

I attributed them to my detached retina issues from 2007/2008. Maybe they are related to peri-menopause along with the daily migraines and dizziness I deal with now. On the road to a consult. We'll see what we reveal.

Karen

definitely need more Pg. I'm interested to see what my 24hr Genova Hormone panel looks like - should get it back in the next week or so...

### leighrmee — 2013-10-06T10:41:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bhrt-experiences-pls-share.2903/post-97009>

After trying my regular ob/gyn trying to get help for THREE years, FINALLY found the BEST doc. He was an ob/gyn for 21 and understands a woman's body and hormones. I'm on 7 mo. of treatment and could NOT be happier. He LISTENS to me and doesn't just go by labs(his are VERY extensive, btw...). He did a T pellet insertion w/ Anastrazole. I also am on 400mg DHEA and 600 mg oral progesterone/day. He will stick with you and keep tweaking until your levels AND symptoms are better! (patience of JOB with me!) Dr. Scott Shapiro of Dr. Scott's Weight Loss & Wellness, Charlotte, NC area. if anyone needs a GREAT hormone doc, I can't say enough GOOD THINGS! Looking back, I've had imbalances for probably 35 years or more. Oh, the WASTED time!

I'm on 7 mo. of treatment and could NOT be happier. He LISTENS to me and doesn't just go by labs(his are VERY extensive, btw...). He did a T pellet insertion w/ Anastrazole. I also am on 400mg DHEA and 600 mg oral progesterone/day. He will stick with you and keep tweaking until your levels AND symptoms are better! (patience of JOB with me!) Dr. Scott Shapiro of Dr. Scott's Weight Loss & Wellness, Charlotte, NC area. if anyone needs a GREAT hormone doc, I can't say enough GOOD THINGS! Looking back, I've had imbalances for probably 35 years or more. Oh, the WASTED time!

That is so fantastic that you have found a great Doc ....thanks for sharing!

# Thread: One week of trying and failing to do Leptin Rx
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-week-of-trying-and-failing-to-do-leptin-rx.2904/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### rottzilla — 2012-08-07T17:24:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-week-of-trying-and-failing-to-do-leptin-rx.2904/post-43428>

Hi everyone! Iâ€™m new. I posted over on hcg forum. I am 40, 35-40 lbs overweight (lost 50 and kept it off for more than 2 years through hcg dieting and some Rosedale dieting.) Iâ€™m also low iron and trying to get pregnant. My doc has me taking iron supplements and I get retested in 2 months.

So, there is a lot going on for me. On the one hand, as a WAP member and paleo believer, I am excited by Dr. Kruseâ€™s protocol. On the other, itâ€™s very important I have certain things I need to do and eat for my iron levels and also for my pregnancy chances and to get my body in tip top shape for a baby. First and foremost, as people on here helped me realize, is getting myself baby ready. Losing weight is second, but itâ€™s very important to me as I have a bad back and sciatic issue that really kicked me in the pants recently. I really need to lose the weight and stay in shape for my health. Iâ€™m only 40 but some days I feel 70.

One thing I really feel I need to eat daily is raw milk. Harvard studies have shown fertility increases by 80% in women that consume 2 servings of dairy daily. Since Iâ€™m tying to do ketogenic paleo FTW (ha ha as people on here have said â€“ love that! But milk is high carbâ€¦ so Iâ€™m using my raw pastured milk and turning it into kefir. According to Domâ€™s site, kefir only has 11 g of carb per cup, so that seems OK. However, it changes the nutritional profile of the fatâ€¦ so Iâ€™m skimming the fat, fermenting it, and then adding the cream back when I drink it. So, each day I drink 1.5 cups of kefir and .5cups of cream with my BAB. I also eat 2 eggs, chicken sausage, fermented beets and red cabbage (for the iron and Vit C). Iâ€™m getting my 50 g of protein and about 26 g of carb (20 net) and 683 calories. For lunch, Iâ€™ll have some red meat and green juice (one cuke, half zucchini, lettuce, 2 celery, and cilantro) and half an avocado. Now Iâ€™m up to 45 g carb (27 net) and 91 g protein, 1100 calories. For dinner, I am just not hungry at all. I may force myself to eat, but sometimes I just go to bed. When I eat, Iâ€™ll have another bit of steak or something, maybe coconut oil or a few nuts.

Help me out here people, Iâ€™m not losing an ounce. What do I need to do? How about the CT? I am preparing by taking cold baths, but only from the tap, no ice yet, or cold showers. I do have to exercise for my recovery but most days I just walk. Iâ€™ve built up over 2 months to 45 min walking now, at a slow pace. Iâ€™ll be doing some yoga and stretching also soon.

Itâ€™s hard because itâ€™s physical therapy recovery from sciatica, preparing for baby, and losing weight. Theyâ€™re not in direct opposition to each other, but they are somewhat (exercise â€“ JK says no â€“ eating milk high carb â€“ and low carb ketogenic)

Maybe I should move my milk to later in the day before I exercise to burn off the carbs? Thoughts? JK says not to exercise first thing in the am, but also says to eat most of your carbs with breakfast.

### [email redacted] — 2012-08-07T17:42:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-week-of-trying-and-failing-to-do-leptin-rx.2904/post-43430>

One thing to think about. . ..

The Harvard Study you are referencing - did you go look at the how the study was designed and what the subjects were eating compared to the control group? Question everything!

The increase in fertility was for SAD eaters that added in the prescribed amount of CAFO dairy. I'm way more pro-dairy (raw, right cows, local and humane conditions etc) than most on here. But by no means do I think it necessary. Also milk itself can be an issue due to carbs and other things for someone inflamed (stubborn fat is inflamed somewhere) . . .you can get lots of the good milky vits and benefits with the raw Heavy Whipping Cream and the butter (fav on here is kerrygold). As far as getting ready for baby, I think the appropriate dairy fats to provide the k2 and A and all that isn't a terrible way to go.

Making Kefir with the raw milk, can also be a great fermented/good bacteria option. But, if you are sensitive enough to the the dairy proteins and they inflame you, then it's a no-no to you.

First off, stop counting calories. Provide your body with nutrients and you body will adjust to the calories. . .you can't possibly adjust your calories to your body on any given day. There is simply no way to judge that! It's kind of magical that way.

Oh, yes, CT CT CT! It's nice that we are heading to the end of summer - gives you an opportunity to work in slowly then hit it as the weather changes!

### rottzilla — 2012-08-07T17:56:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-week-of-trying-and-failing-to-do-leptin-rx.2904/post-43432>

Thanks for the thoughtful reply… in the Harvard study, the women who consumed full fat dairy had 80% increase in fertility as opposed to those who did not consume dairy, and oddly enough, (I’m forgetting exact number) but something like a 70% decrease in fertility (due to ovulatory disorder) for women consuming reduced fat dairy. It came out of the nurse’s health study.

I hadn’t thought of the inflammation component, which is odd because I’m a big proponent of inflammation being a marker of all disease. I do eat a fermented CLO and about to add liver for the Vit A and D. Raw cream is my preference but I can’t get it out here. Mass doesn’t allow farmers to sell their raw cream unless it’s to a producer. My farmer used to have us sign contracts saying we were not the end users and their cream was being put to manufacture in something else, but now they are just tired of the games and won’t sell cream at all. I’ve found pastured cream, but it’s not raw.

OK, I’ll move faster on the CT and also stop worrying about calories. I’ll really have to start to pay attention to how the dairy is affecting me and maybe stop it for a week or two and see my results and then compare to when I do drink it.

### [email redacted] — 2012-08-07T18:10:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-week-of-trying-and-failing-to-do-leptin-rx.2904/post-43437>

Also, just for the record - I commiserate on how annoying and frustrating it is to "do all this" and really put the effort into a lifestyle that is supposed to "fix" things, and still be stuck with the weight! Sounds like you should focus on training and therapy for your back pain issue. . . as you don't want to be in pain the entire time - that can't be good for either of you (think inflamation!) and it sounds like you are way above the crowd on eating, too - I bet this works out

I was the last person in line as a candidate for parenthood, so before getting preggers I did nothing for fertility or preparing body to make a good babe. . but since then have learned alot trying to have a 2nd (that one didn't happen, irony - thanks nature) One thing you never see in all the fertility stuff is the timing of your orgasm while sperms are all up in there! Summary: He should go first, and you right after! Internal contractions of the lady parts pull the swimmers up.

Hi Rottzilla,

it's great to read your post! I am in your same boat (I am AMA and also trying to get pregnant, well, get ready for it as my hormones are all screwed up and I am in AF, low thyroid etc). I don't actually do dairy because I also have to recover from leaky gut except butter (Kerrygold) though I hope to introduce some raw goat cheese (to test).....so that is great you can do dairy. I love raw cream!

Do you know about this Amish Farmer in PA? They will ship to you, if you become a member, for I think $25 a year....it's not cheap for overnight shipping but they have raw cream....in fact they have a ton of stuff and it's all grass fed. They are wonderful people. I saw they have a co-op in Concord Mass, not sure how far that is for you. You might want to check it out.  I highly recommend their stuff and have used them on and off for a few years, especially for soy-free, pastured eggs which are so hard to find....

Here is the link: [http://millersorganicfarm.com/millers-co-ops-in-us/](http://http://millersorganicfarm.com/millers-co-ops-in-us/)

Good luck. And by the way I would love to connect with you offline....please keep posting here with your experience.

### Shijin13 — 2012-09-02T18:41:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-week-of-trying-and-failing-to-do-leptin-rx.2904/post-48973>

Ditch the dairy for one month see what happens. Yes you'll probably crave it but it's possible you gut flora is so crazy that is driving your need. Also dairy acts just like an opiate and attached to the same receptors. We don't need dairy. You'll find as you increase your seafood consumption you won't want the dairy.

I love the taste of all dairy (or at least what I remember of it) but I totally agree with Shijin on this... it was driving my cravings up. MamaGrok has pointed out that dairy cravings can be K2 cravings, so maybe supplement that. But the switch to epi-paleo will undoubtedly help you, not just with cravings and feeling better, but with getting pregnant and getting hormones where they need to be. Good luck!

# Thread: Great Meatball Trick!!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/great-meatball-trick.2907/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 26
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### [email redacted] — 2012-08-07T18:41:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/great-meatball-trick.2907/post-43440>

I made the best meatloaf/meat ball stuff last night. Had a few tricks up my sleeve - got the idea from this months cooks illustrated.

2 lbds of meat (I used ground pork and ground bison)

1 onion finely chopped

Boatload of garlic finely chopped (I never feel there is enough, do as much as you can stand)

3 tbs coconut flakes

about 4-6 anchovies chopped fine and rinsed of olive oil.

1/2 cup bonebroth

1 packet of unflavored gelatin

seasonings to your liking. (I did italian as we were having spaghetti squash)

Saute onion on lower temps till starts to brown. I used red palm oil because I had some. This step takes longer than one thinks it should.

Add garlic and anchovies once onion starts to turn brown, keep sauting until garlic is cooked

Warm broth, dissolve gelatin in it.

In a bowl mix: meat, coconut, sauted veggies, broth/gelatin and season to taste!

I baked at 350 in little meatballs and a few meatloafts. AWESOME!!!! These are so juicy and moist and savory tasting!

### ealachan — 2012-08-08T19:09:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/great-meatball-trick.2907/post-43686>

> **Souldanzer;43974 wrote: I asked that question and Dr K said bone broth doesn't have glutamate...... I have been reacting to bone broth since my brain injury from my skiing accident. Can't have it. Bone broth = un-imaginable headache, brain fog and day spent horizontal with ice packs in bed.

>
>

**Bone broth not having glutamates does not jive, though. Glutamic acid is a building block of protein, so it occurs in anything that contains protein.

I'm not arguing that MSG is bad \*\*\*\*, btw...I'm just trying to understand how we're connecting the dots between gelatin and MSG. It seems to me that, if one is not over-sensitive to glutamates, that the naturally** occurring glutamates in bone broth and the resultant gelatins would not be a bad thing, but rather quite beneficial.

### Souldanzer — 2012-08-08T19:12:25+0300

It seems to me that, if one is not over-sensitive to glutamates, that the naturally** occurring glutamates in bone broth and the resultant gelatins would not be a bad thing, but rather quite beneficial.

any meat you eat has glutamate.... I think it's a concentration issue. Bone broth is good, I believe that. The long cooking process probably concentrates the glutamate contained and therefore, someone like me with lots of brain issues and sensitivities, might react.

Gelatin is commercially processed at probably high temps or something like that..... WAPF has the answer why it should be avoided but my brain is leaving me here

### [email redacted] — 2012-08-08T19:44:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/great-meatball-trick.2907/post-43690>

on another tangent, the reason for adding the gelatin in the first place. . .

The best meatloafs are usually made with a panade, which is a milk/bread paste that helps create a lighter juicier loaf. With NO panade what happens is liquids are not trapped, and the ground meat proteins binds tightly, squeezing out the moisture. This is made worse the more you "work" the meat and roll it and squeeze it.

They used mushrooms and gelatin (I used coconut flakes as an experiment cause I didn't have mushrooms on hand). THe sponge like mushrooms and gelatin trap moisture and lubricate the fibers - supposedly distributing flavors and juicyness.

My N-1 was that the meat balls made this way - with gelatin, were WAAAAY moister, fluffier and tastier than without fwiw. I did mix mine with actual bone broth too.

I am going to "reduce" some bone broth to create that natural gelatine like goop and use that with the finely chopped mushrooms on the next batch.

Has anybody made lots of fish stock? Do you get the same jello-goo from that if you cook it down?

### Glamazon — 2012-08-09T16:35:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/great-meatball-trick.2907/post-43892>

> **ealachan;43958 wrote:** But home-made bone broth often has gelatin in it from the bones being cooked so long. In fact, it's considered a desirable trait in bone broth, is it not?

My bone broth is really stock. I don't care for the taste of plain bones. I take something like oxtails, cover in a liquid in a crockpot with a splash of apple cider vinegar and cook for 8-12 hours. The tendons and cartilage break down into yummy jelly. I cook it so long I end up throwing away the meat because it tastes like cardboard. When it cools you can cut it like custard. I add spoonfuls of it to when I am cooking or I make "beef tea" for sipping.

If I eat a cup a day of it, like I do for long stretches in the winter, my body temp gets very warm and I get really horny. This is why I don't eat it in the spring because it raises my body temp till I sweat for hours. I use non hormone meat and mostly grassfed all the time from WFs or I order it. In Chinese medicine this kind of broth is meant to build jing chi. Jing is body energy that is most likely related to what westerners call hormones. It is given to the sick and the elderly for rejuvenation. Sometimes a knob of ginger is added because Ginger is considered an anti aging tonic. In China and here in the US, shaving fresh ginger into boiling water, let cook for about 5 minutes, then strain and drink it as considered a daily longevity tonic. A lot of the chinese elderly drink it.

This bone stock has a lot of Methylsulfonylmethane (MSM) and natural cartilage in it.

I have no bad reaction to this stuff at all.

### [email redacted] — 2012-08-11T17:46:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/great-meatball-trick.2907/post-44333>

> **Anna K.;44637 wrote:** It would be great to know, because according to great lakes gelatin's link, if I understand it correctly, normally processed gelatin has no MSG. It doesn't look like they are using any special methods to be msg free.
>
>
>
> so I also don't see how it's different from bone broth.

I looked through WAPF pretty hard and didn't see anything that would conflict with what the Great Lakes site has. I am wondering still on bone broth vs gelatin.

### janagram — 2012-08-11T18:29:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/great-meatball-trick.2907/post-44345>

Glam, what did you mean when you said you make stock because you don't care for bone broth? (always wondered what the difference was)

### Glamazon — 2012-08-13T04:00:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/great-meatball-trick.2907/post-44617>

> **janagram;44653 wrote:** Glam, what did you mean when you said you make stock because you don't care for bone broth? (always wondered what the difference was)

It depends on the region and culture. Some use the term interchangeably. I once ordered bone broth from grass fed meat supplier. It tasted like bones. Basically, the bones are just used. In stock, there is more than bones used, like carcasses and veggies. Broth is usually strained and clear. It can gel up but I find the way I make stock is much more a richer and thicker result. The gel cuts like custard. I have yet to make fish stock so I cant speak to that. Boiling pig parts like hocks makes a rich stock very quick, faster than a beef or chicken stock. I can put some hocks on and simmer for three hours and have something that takes 8-12 hours for beef. I cook the beef for so long everything dissolves if I include veggies, especially onions. Restaurant quality stock or broth is clear. I eat it without straining it.

An interesting side note, chitin, which comes from shrimp shells (or any crustacean) is used in surgery and procedures to close up wounds because it is absorb-able and tough and resilient. It can also help accelerate wound healing when applied.

### jib — 2012-08-08T02:53:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-lalanne.2910/post-43539>

ah yah......I think after he passed dr eades did a short on him either via his blog or twitter and he figured that for part of his life Lalanne did some steak (meat) in there somewhere before the "infomercial" juicing thing came about. Maybe the juicing shortened his life, I don't really remember what the media said about this. But he sure made a lot of difference in some of the people who followed him on the TV. I don't remember him talking too much about nutrition "back in the day", but it seems his message then was to do "something" to engage your muscles. Oh yeah, lose some weight too!!

### AKMan — 2012-08-08T03:08:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-lalanne.2910/post-43545>

Two of his often-quoted sayings were: "If man made it--don't eat it" and "If it tastes good, spit it out".

He is living proof that a simple diet and exercise will get you at least to 97. He has a heart valve replacement at 93 and died 4 years later of complications with pneumonia. Amazing what a guy like him accomplished without the internet or access to all the science we have now, then again, maybe we could all learn from that.

I firmly believe that epi-paleo, CT, and strict adherence to circadian cycles will get people easily into the 100's.

I have always been impressed with Jack LaLanne. He was just so far ahead of everyone else. I remember hearing him in an interview at age 92, talking about his daily routine. "Up with the sun, swimming in a cold pool followed by a high protein breakfast of eggs, a little fruit and green juice. Gives me all the energy I need and to spare!" I'm paraphrasing of course. I also liked that he said it was important to always have big goals in life and that he had his goals mapped out until he reached 115...then maybe he would consider "retirement".

### AKMan — 2012-08-08T20:19:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-lalanne.2910/post-43699>

I posted this on MDA this morning, enjoy...

This was an awesome thread, thanks to whoever started it. I spent all day yesterday and this morning reading and watching Jack Lalanne videos--he is truly an inspiration. Here's a great interview he did in 2003.

[http://www.shareguide.com/LaLanne.html](http://http://www.shareguide.com/LaLanne.html)

Best quotes:

"That's what I am saying. You have to take care of your 640 muscles, and the number one thing is exercise. You can eat perfectly but if you don't exercise, you cannot get by. There are so many health food nuts out there that eat nothing but natural foods but they don't exercise and they look terrible. Then there are other people who exercise like a son-of-a-gun but eat a lot of junk. **They look pretty good because the exercise is king. Nutrition is queen. Put them together and you've got a kingdom!"

"Share Guide: Many people who go to the gym only go three days a week rather than six or seven. What do you think about this?

Jack LaLanne: Three days is enough if it's vigorous.

Share Guide: I go to the gym in the morning before work, around 7 am.

Jack LaLanne: Do you work with the weights?

Share Guide: Sometimes. I start with 30 minutes on the treadmill or stairmaster.

Jack LaLanne: Twelve to seventeen minutes is plenty on the treadmill--if it's done fast. That's all you need for cardiovascular benefit. You don't need to spend that extra time unless you are over weight and you need to burn off extra calories.

Share Guide: How fast do I need to go on the treadmill?

Jack LaLanne: Do it vigorously, like somebody is chasing you. You've got to do it hard. Otherwise, if you just take it easy and do it longer, you are spending all that time when you don't need it. Use that extra time with your weights instead. Focus on your problem zones, your strength, your energy, your flexibility and all the rest. Maybe your chest is flabby or your hips or waist need toning. Also, you should change your program every thirty days. That's the key."

"Jack LaLanne: I say if you enjoy it, do it. Let me ask this: when you are at the gym, how long do you rest before you do the next exercise?

Share Guide: Generally I do two sets of 10-12 reps with a minute in between.

Jack LaLanne: That's too long. I rest ten seconds between sets.

Share Guide: Two sets per exercise?

Jack LaLanne: That is the very minimum. I do 20 sets for deltoids, 20 sets for pecs, and 20 sets for lats. It's important to change things around. Maybe for 30 days I might do ten reps with high weight, but the tenth rep should be to failure."

"Share Guide: Why did you stop being a strict vegetarian?

Jack LaLanne: In those days everybody was saying that you had to eat meat to build muscle, so I went on a meat thing for awhile. NowI only eat fish--no chicken, no turkey, just fish. **I get all my protein from fish and egg whites.**

Share Guide: That is what my favorite healing authority says, Dr. Andrew Weil. He believes in eating fish and dairy on top of the vegetarian diet--the vegaquarian diet.

Jack LaLanne: I use no milk of any kind. Anything that comes from a cow I don't eat.**

Share Guide: You aren't eating nonfat yogurt with your fruit anymore?

Jack LaLanne: I may take one bite. Or a little skim milk once in a while is not going to hurt you. It isn't what you do once in a while that's a problem; it's what you do all the time. Look at the average American diet: ice cream, butter, cheese, whole milk, all this fat. People don't realize how much of this stuff you get by the end of the day. High blood pressure is from all this high-fat eating. Do you know how many calories are in butter and cheese and ice cream? Would you get your dog up in the morning for a cup of coffee and a donut? Probably millions of Americans got up this morning with a cup of coffee, a cigarette and a donut. No wonder they are sick and fouled up."

"Jack LaLanne: It's a bunch of bull. How do you know what's really organic? Today, there's all these impurities in the water and the air. The water for the fruits and vegetables has junk in it. If you get enough vitamins and minerals out of normal food and whole grains, and you get enough proteins and exercise (that's the key) then nature builds up a tolerance to all of these things. It's survival of the fittest. You can't have everything perfect, that's impossible, but the fit survive. The fit can handle the impurities in the air and in the water, but the poor people who are sick, it really affects them more."

"Jack LaLanne: That's right, don't kid yourself. You have a mirror, right? And you know what you can do. One thing that you have to remember is SCALES LIE! The biggest liars we have are the scales. I always ask in my lectures, "What did you weigh when you were 20 years old?" Someone will say, "Oh, I weighed 170 lbs." But then they played football and basketball, and were in good shape. Now they guy is 50 years old and he says, " Jack, I haven't gained a pound! I weigh 170 just like I did when I was 20!" I'll say "Really, that's wonderful! How big was your waist when you were 20?" "Oh, 30 inches" "How big is it today?" "Oh, 36." The sands of time have shifted. The guy put five or six inches on his waist. You figure about every inch is five pounds approximately. That guy has gained about 30 pounds of fat and lost 30 pounds of muscle and the scale says he weighs the same.

Share Guide: So it's what you are made of not how much you weigh.

Jack LaLanne: Yes. What you need to do is get that tape measure out, and start measuring that gut. Then you start working out and you start eating properly till that gut gets down close to it was when you were in your 20's. Then you'll find out what your weight should be.

Share Guide: Sounds like a lot of sit-ups to me!

Jack LaLanne: It's not sit-ups, it's everything! In fact, if you've got a big gut and you start doing sit-ups, you are going to get bigger because you build up the muscle. You've got to get rid of that fat! How do you get rid of fat? By changing your diet. You can't get rid of it with exercise alone. You can do the most vigorous exercise and only burn up 300 calories in an hour. If you've got fat on your body, the exercise firms and tones the muscles. But when you use that tape measure, what makes it bigger? It's the fat! A two or three inch layer of fat. The only way you get that fat off is to eat less and exercise more.

Share Guide: Is that is what you mean by saying "your waistline is your lifeline?"

Jack LaLanne: Yes. Getting out of shape is like a thief in the night that sneaks up on you. I always tell people that it is never too late. I tell them about the folks in their 90's that doubled their strength and endurance. In the old days the doctors would say: "Slow down Jack, you're going to have a heart attack; you won't be able to get an erection; you'll make the women look like men." They thought that athletes that worked out with my system wouldn't be able to throw a ball because they'd be too muscle bound. Those are the misconceptions I had to go through for about 40 years. Now the medical profession is saying something different. Every one of the doctors without exception is saying, "Build muscle." Things really changed after I went on television and got the message out. That's when I starting reaching millions of people. In fact, I just made The Guinness Book of World Records for being on television 34 years non-stop (20 years in Europe)."

"Jack LaLanne: You have to be pretty stupid to think there isn't a Supreme Being to put all this together. Do you think that man could ever make a heart that is indestructible? Do you think that man could make a calculator like your brain? Do you think that man could ever make a machine that the only way you could hurt it is don't use it? Could man make a machine that in every 90 days practically every cell changes? Think about this. Look at all the creatures, the snakes, the worms and the birds, and the entire universe and everything that goes on. Don't you think there is a universal power, that there is some Supreme Being that put this all together? You don't have to call it God or Jesus. That's religious humbug to a lot of people, but you've gotta believe that nature and spiritual things surround us. That is what put us here! I thank the universe for that every day of my life. We don't know all the answers. If we knew all the answers we'd be bored, wouldn't we? We keep looking, searching, trying to get more knowledge. I'm just trying to help people with my lectures. I say, "You are out on the sea and drowning yourselves. Here is a life preserver. Grab it. I can save your life. Help yourself!"

# Thread: Hello from Kentucky!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-kentucky.2912/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### adameads — 2012-08-08T05:29:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-kentucky.2912/post-43585>

I've had great success with the Primal Blueprint, but am looking for something to give me an extra boost. I came across Dr. Kruse on a podcast (fatburningman) while sleeping last night, and I spent most of today listening to Dr. Kruse's various speeches/talks/interviews on YouTube/iTunes/etc. Needless to say, I'm VERY intrigued.

Is it really as simple as continuing to follow the Primal Blueprint diet and adding CT?

I did a five minute bath tonight using full-cold tap water. It's been hot here, so I don't think the water is as cold as it could be. It wasn't that difficult. I've always been a fan of ending my showers with cold water in the summer because, at least in my head, it slams shut the pores. I seem to sweat less when I do this...maybe now I'll discover the true answer as to why?

A little about me:

6'1"

31 years old

SW: 251 (12/28/2011)

CW: 213 (have been as low as 207)

The last month my diet has been bad because of a move to a new home, busier at work (overnights at hotels), etc. When I'm eating clean, it's eggs and meat or leftovers for breakfast. Lunch/dinner consists of some of the following: GF beef, pastured pork/chicken, sardines, eggs, avocado, seasonal veggies from our CSA, a little fruit. I eat a Larabar three or four days a week. I take 10,000 IU of D3 three or four days a week, and 5,000 IU the rest.

Looking forward to learning from the community.

Thanks!

Adam

### Kiera2 — 2012-08-08T11:55:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-kentucky.2912/post-43609>

I came across Dr. Kruse on a podcast (fatburningman) while sleeping last night, and I spent most of today listening to Dr. Kruse's various speeches/talks/interviews on YouTube/iTunes/etc. Needless to say, I'm VERY intrigued.
>
>
>
> Is it really as simple as continuing to follow the Primal Blueprint diet and adding CT?

Hi Adam, welcome! I think the answer is that it depends on where you're starting from, and what your goals are. This recent blog from Jack shows how to eat optimally [http://jackkruse.com/brain-gut-6-epi-paleo-rx/](http://http://jackkruse.com/brain-gut-6-epi-paleo-rx/) Good luck!

### Inger — 2012-08-08T10:35:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-are-those-muscles-coming-from-tho.2914/post-43606>

Yes. My sisters commented I look so strong, when we was eating out the other day. As I tried on this skirt I got from my sister, she did not wanted it anymore.. then I realized it is true. Am I turning into a man slowly..?! All this tanning and fish and minor CT (just a short swim in the river instead of showering, and 3 min. facedunks and icepack under my head by night) are doing something.

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

...

I have zero exercise and live sedentary, actually I have started to do about 5 minutes swings with quite light weights for my butt and something minor like 5 minutes for shoulders / week, but it is so little it should not have any impact..?

It might be the wormy mackerel-heads for sure.. LOL

PS. this was today morning after a really bad nights sleep. I had two cups of coffee yesterday and that was a bad mistake, then also huge stress otherwise.. I usually sleep really well tho, better than ever before!

Something is happening in my brain too.. that I love. It is kind of I feel more and I find so nice solutions about many things..! And listening to a very nice song today made me almost explode from the feelings I got! This is totally drug free too - I never had drugs but I can imagine this might be what it feels like..LOL.. I made connections in my mind that I have not before and it makes me so very happy!!!! They are very minor changes but at the same time so huge to me!!! Wow.

### tweety — 2012-08-08T15:35:18+0300

### Inger — 2012-08-08T16:57:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-are-those-muscles-coming-from-tho.2914/post-43654>

Thank you all, it does not look like you think I am turning manly tho.. that makes me happy because sure I like to be strong! Maybe I should start do some more exercise too than 10 minutes / week..

Nonchalant, I do so little CT! Just max 10 minutes or less / day in the river (17 degrees C right now) and icepack under my head before sleeping and facedunks 3 minutes every morning. If it is the CT I wonder what will happen when winter comes and I go in the icecold river every day.. huh!

Tweety, I have been eating high raw for many years, yes. Like most days 90 % raw maybe? I eat meat almost only raw, fish also cooked, but also raw. I do try to eat only wild foods, like elk meat and wildcaught fish and such. Lots and lots of coconutoil, maybe 5 spoons a day. Some days less.

CT, MHS, dark in the evenings.. all these are new for me. I have messed up my hormones real bad I figure, doing late and very stressing work for many many years, living 13 years with a husband that abused alkohol.. and staying in front of the mirror late at night with strong lights in my eyes.. picking pimples.. So stupid! But it was a must for me.. until I read Kruse, and found out it was destroing me. I had bad skin always and picking the pimples in front of the mirror at night at 11-12 PM, alone, "helped" my anxiousness (sure not but I thought so). I also used the computer late in bed, like after 11 PM.

I do eat a lot. If I have been eating much too late (what does not happend often) I still always eat a BAB in the morning, even if not hungry at all. I eat until stuffed and I easily get 60-80 g protein, absolutely not a problem for me. I do not count calories at all but eats as much as I like to, I never go hungry and eat 3 times a day.

I think I've got something similar going on but too early to tell.....

### Inger — 2012-08-09T07:52:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-are-those-muscles-coming-from-tho.2914/post-43837>

> **caroline;44122 wrote:** Inger - have you got the low blue light glasses? I have found they have made a huge difference

Caroline, I do use normal sunglasses. I picked some cheap one but dark and brownish in color. They work well I guess, I use them when I read my computer at night. I have f.lux and the screen as dark as it gets, and sunglasses on. Works for reading, not for writing but I only read so it is fine. I know it is fine because I get soooo tired quite soon, I read about max half an hour and then I cannot keep my eyes open anymore. I sleep well too and wake up around 6 AM every morning.

I use my dark sunglasses when in the train at my way home from work in the evening, I use them when I am out meeting friends.. I use them on parties.. everywhere where they use artificial lights at night. People stare.. (I guess they might think I am a junkie.. LOL) and my siblings wondered why too but then I explain... I heal my hormones. I tell it to everyone that asks. Very cool. Healing hormones. I think it sounds great!

I am happy to tell people about the protocol, maybe they will too one day, step onto the way to optimal!

### Dali Dula — 2012-08-09T14:50:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-are-those-muscles-coming-from-tho.2914/post-43860>

I found that when i started not eating for four hours before bed and going to bed early between 9-10 the muscle growth and fat loss was remarkable. I could actually feel it when I woke up. And it just keeps getting better. My weight has not changed, but my body comp has changed dramatically. Oh, and I feel great. Still haven't tried fish head smoothie.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-08-09T15:08:21+0300

You choose when you eat.......why blame him! Eat when you should leave his dinner on the plate when he gets home he can eat when he wants and his life can slowly fall into the toilet on his own accord. Do not let his behavior affect your health. If you do you have no one to blame but yourself. You have to exercise control over your domain and stop letting it be usurped by excuses.

Tough message but man you need to hear it and get it.

### Souldanzer — 2012-08-09T18:55:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-are-those-muscles-coming-from-tho.2914/post-43913>

> **Jack Kruse;44157 wrote:** You choose when you eat.......why blame him! Eat when you should leave his dinner on the plate when he gets home he can eat when he wants and his life can slowly fall into the toilet on his own accord. Do not let his behavior affect your health. If you do you have no one to blame but yourself. You have to exercise control over your domain and stop letting it be usurped by excuses.
>
>
>
> Tough message but man you need to hear it and get it.

+10000...... had to do that during AN re-feeding. Partner hated it but I had to eat when I needed to eat.... which I had no control over at the time. Body was very demanding of food when it needed it. Still is. Friends don't like it either b/c it makes it hard to go out for dinner with me. Girl's gotta eat when she gets hungry..... no way around it. I suggest they adapt to \*my\* schedule since they seem to have more flexibility in their eating patterns. Lol.... Yet have to see that happen.....

V, it's what I mean with a partner shouldn't hold you back from thriving in life..... you can't force someone to change and you can't force yourself NOT to change. I had to allow this to happen.... not with the thought of leaving my partner at all... more like, I need to take care of myself and let's see how things will work out. Eventually, my partner and I were living separate lives with a lot of things..... food being a major one. It's about finding your voice and your power. Some relationships take well to it and evolve....move to the next level.... some do not. No way of telling. Food is not the only place that you are evolving. You're evolving as a person, on the inside, your desires and dreams..... everywhere.

### Inger — 2012-08-09T19:35:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-are-those-muscles-coming-from-tho.2914/post-43920>

Vkiernan,

me and hubby always ate when we wanted it, and what we wanted. This freedom needs to be. I used to make him dinner and then sit by him whe he was eating, just for companionship. He did not cared if I ate or not! And no.. that is not why we separated  (he do is thinking about us beeing together soon but I need to heal totally first before I can decide that, he respects that very much. I am not going into the same stress (alcohol abuse) ever again for sure).

You could do that too, just sit by him if he does not want to eat alone!

### KiwiLauren — 2012-08-10T00:26:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-are-those-muscles-coming-from-tho.2914/post-43972>

> **vkiernan;44224 wrote:** Yep, that is where I fail big time; I let others run me over. Maybe that is why I love my BAB so much. He is still sleeping and it is all about me and what I want. No one else to consider and no conflict. Can you see why I have AF now? I hate conflict! Some things take me forever to get "out." Guess I need to hold a meeting and discuss "my" eating habits and how they need to change for me. My health thanks you for the kick!

V, I don't think you need to discuss anything. Just move into action. There is no case to be made or proven here. A marriage doesn't depend on synchronised eating schedules but your health depends on you eating at the right times. I pretty much never eat with my children or husband, but I always sit at the table with them and have conversation time. (It's hard to engage in conversation anyway while gulping down raw fish smoothies.) This seems an area where a belief you have ("I hate conflict") is holding you hostage and actually creating exactly that which you hate (LOTS of conflict, both internally and unspoken externally). You are very open and brave to identify what is going on here. But instead of analysing it to death and discussing it ad nauseum (with a partner who may or may not get it), don your Optimal Superhero suit and move into action. You'll be great! Promise.

### tweety — 2012-08-10T00:31:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-are-those-muscles-coming-from-tho.2914/post-43974>

I have found it very challenging to balance this. We eat without my husband a lot of nights, but it's eating without the kids that bugs me. I have always placed a lot of value on family meal time, so I struggle when they eat without parents at the table.

I overcame it for lunch, and I just sit with them and drink water. Breakfast they are usually with me. But dinner is the big one!

Itdoes bother me when my hub doesn't come home in time, but I just move on without him. I'm still irritated w him, but much less than if I had waited to have dinner when he came home! There's emotional connection for sure sitting down to a meal together.

### [email redacted] — 2012-08-10T00:37:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-are-those-muscles-coming-from-tho.2914/post-43975>

But instead of analysing it to death and discussing it ad nauseum (with a partner who may or may not get it), don your Optimal Superhero suit and move into action. You'll be great! Promise.

Well V, I hope all this commentary is welcome - I think we mean well! I do mean well with this!!!!

I have to agree with Kiwi on this. In addition, could this "poor me, I suffer so at his hand" be your modus operandi on actually retaining control and trying to manipulate him? Like if the crying in bed doesn't get his attention - perhaps being sick with "see I told you so!" thrown in will do it?

That "agressively meek" interaction will really wear on a strong personality and can set up a very manipulative and unpleasant dynamic for all involved. By "not asserting" yourself you are abusing him in a sense. . . Mates DO want to please, and if you choose NOT to run your life like you want it - they don't like feeling that resentfulness about it. People want a partner in crime, not just someone that does what they want and loves em no matter how they behave. . . unless it's their mommy.

### vkiernan — 2012-08-10T02:15:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-are-those-muscles-coming-from-tho.2914/post-44007>

Yes, feedback is always welcome. I am not always a push over. Many months ago I got fed up with waiting for him to decide what he wanted to eat (this was happening a lot and hours were passing by me and I was fricken hungry) and I got pissed off and just got up and made my own meal and sat down next to him and ate it. He was upset for sure and that is why I try to eat with him if he is coming home before 8. Didn't get my point across either. He does know I need to eat early as we talk about it enough and some days are definately better than others. It is not all bad. You guys just get the bad stuff. He isn't a bad guy, he just has very screwed up eating and sleeping habits and yes he will pay them later down the road. And yes, I do go to bed myself everynight without him.

You all have convinced me I am not a bad wife if I eat by myself. I guess this might be part of my hang up. I do have to let him know cuz that wouldn't be fair to just do it and not let him know. Whoever said I was over-analysing it is way more correct than you know. That is part of my job as an accountant and I am damn good at it. Unfortunately, it bleeds into my personal life and I analyze the hell out of that too.

Cavemam, this is diff. than the crying - that is damn hormones getting the better of me. I never throw eating late in his face, I just let him know I am hungry and gotta eat. Most days the words "I am not eating at 8pm" comes out of my mouth when discussing dinner if he makes it sound like it will be later. He knows I hate eating late.

Jack, I'm guessing the point you are making by me as an example is NOT good one but if it helps someone else than so be it. I know I need help or I wouldn't be out here everyday of my life!

### Inger — 2012-08-10T07:38:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-are-those-muscles-coming-from-tho.2914/post-44080>

My own N1.

Men loves women who just do their thing, and then are happy and satisfied. Confident, strong women. Who never complain. What is there to complain about, when you do what you want / need? Men loves when women are positive and happy about life! Who cares about eating patterns? Only womens.. AFAK. I eat strange food all the time and on my own and it never bothered any men I met, ever. They want you to be happy!  And yeah.. maybe make them some delicious food once in a while.. get some kisses and a smile..

That's why I love men so much, they are pretty uncomplicated. At last the men I met in my life.

Vkiernan, I can see in front of my inner eye how your marriage is changing.. in a very happy way!
